activity_name: market for aluminium removed by milling, small parts
reference_product_name: aluminium removed by milling, small parts
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'aluminium removed by milling, small parts'. Milling is a chipping process where the piece of the material is fixed to a movable table while the cutting energy is provided by the rotating cutting tool. It is used for complex shaping and it can be applied to metals, wood and plastic materials. This dataset represents the milling of 1kg of aluminium in small parts. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, energy consumption, water consumption and materials end-of-life. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

The product "mastic asphalt" represents a mixture of bitumen and aggregates. The raw materials per kg ready mix product are mixed in following ratio: Limestone powder (0.260 kg), sand (0.66 kg), bitumen (0.060 kg) and natural bitumen (0.020 kg). Mastic asphalt is defined by the Mastic Asphalt Council1 as follows: Mastic asphalt is a type of asphalt composed of graded mineral matter and asphaltic cement in such proportions as to form a cohesive, impermeable mass. It is solid or semi-solid under normal temperature conditions, but sufficiently fluid when brought to a suitable temperature to be spread by means of a hand float or by mechanical means without compaction. Mastic asphalt is used as protection for a wide range of construction applications. It offers total water impermeability for roofing and tanking, acts as a working surface and damp-proof membrane in flooring and is ideal for paving, offering a durable wearing surface.

activity_name: market for cleft timber, measured as dry mass
reference_product_name: cleft timber, measured as dry mass
product_information: The product 'cleft timber, measured as dry mass' represents a mix of cleft timber produced from residual softwood and hardwood from forestry activities as well as cleft timber produced as the sole reference product from planted eucalyptus forest management activities. The amount is expressed as dry mass, including bark. Production takes place in the forest. When produced from residual softwood and hardwood, the wood is cut into one-meter-long log pieces. The logs are split along the grain with a mobile, tractor-powered clefting device. When produced from eucalyptus hardwood, the production of cleft timber includes the use of a grapple saw or a chain saw. Cleft timber is used as wood fuel with a lower heating value of circa 14.46 MJ/kg.
Polyester fibre is a synthetic fibre that is extensively used in the textile industry. Polyethylene terephthalate is the most common polyester.

Ammonium Paratungstate (APT) has the chemical formula (NH4)10(H2W12O42) 4H2O and is the most important intermediate product for all tungsten products. 
Lassner, E., Schubert, W.-D., Lüderitz, E., Wolf, H.U., 2000. Tungsten, Tungsten Alloys, and Tungsten Compounds. In 'Ullmann’s Encyclopedia of Industrial Chemistry'. Wiley-VCH Verlag GmbH & Co. KGaA, Weinheim, Germany, pp. 1–53. https://doi.org/10.1002/14356007.a27_229

activity_name: market for steel removed by turning, primarily dressing, conventional
reference_product_name: steel removed by turning, primarily dressing, conventional
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'steel removed by turning, primarily dressing, conventional'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. When the process is performed with dressing operation, then only minor amounts of materials are removed to produce the exact dimensions and smooth surface. The process is conventional, meaning that it is controlled manually and needs constant supervision. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of steel. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, hot, tempering'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. To further strengthen and harden the extruded metal, tempering process is used via quenching (cooling), heat treatment, and/or cold working techniques. This process represents the tempering of 1 kg steel and it is considered as an integral part of the hot impact extrusion dataset. It only includes the energy consumption for the heat treatment of the steel after it has been deformed by impact extrusion.

product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of aluminium, 4 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes it is possible to produce complex shapes. This dataset represents the impact extrusion of 1 kg of aluminium with 4 strokes. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but is excludes the input of alumimium and the process of degreasing is not included.

polyvinylfluoride, film', is a plastic product of fossil origin, it is not biodegradable and it is a thermoplastic material. This product consists of 100% virgin material with no content of recycled material. The product is used in the following applications and sectors: Poly(vinyl fluoride) films are laminated to sheets of metal with special adhesives. The laminates are used for exterior siding on industrial and residential buildings. Pigmented films can be made in any colour. These films made from PVF have exceptional resistance to sunlight, chemical attack, and water adsorption. They are also extremely tough and have good abrasion resistance. Films can also be laminated to hardboard, paper, polystyrene, polyurethane, and other substrates. These laminates are used for wall coverings, aircraft cabin interiors, pipe covering, duct liners, as well as other applications. A weather-resistant PVF barrier is also resistant to abrasion, chemicals, solvents, and staining. Another application for PVF film is as a release sheeting in the molding of phenolic, epoxide, and polyester plastics, and as a release film for bag molding. It does not become brittle and strips easily from the mold.Toughness is maintained at elevated temperatures at all degrees of humidity and in contact with common solvents. The film has been used for photovoltaic applications because of its high transparency and resistance to solar radiation.

activity_name: market for vacuum infusion moulding for composite product
reference_product_name: vacuum infusion moulding for composite product
product_information:
This is delivering the service of "vacuum infusion moulding for composite product".
Infusion moulding is a process for composite material transformation in which resin is infused using vacuum on the top of the fibres laid on the mould. And then the mixture is left at ambient temperature to cure.
In the service of infusion moulding, 1 kg of this process corresponds to 0.82kg of the final product at the end of the process. The converted amount of material (fibres and resin) is not included into the dataset. The user should list 1kg of the service and 1 kg of the resin and fibre INPUT for an OUTPUT of 0.82 kg of material processed. If an activity requires 1 kg of infused product, the user should list (i) 1/0.82 = 1.22 kg of the service and 1.22 kg of the resin/fibres type processed.

product_information: The product 'palm date, conditioned and dried, organic' represents organic palm date fruit conditioned and dried. The drying process includes the following activities: sorting, washing, freezing, cooling, heating, drying. The conditioning of the organic palm date is different from the conditioning of conventional palm date. The fumigation is replaced by the freezing (4 hours under -20 °C) of the dates and there is no glucosing. This product is produced through organic agricultural practices which do not include fertiliser and pesticide application.

activity_name: market for steel removed by turning, average, computer numerical controlled
reference_product_name: steel removed by turning, average, computer numerical controlled
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'steel removed by turning, average, computer numerical controlled'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. The process is computer numerical controlled, meaning that it operates autonomously once programmed. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of steel. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

Tantalum concentrate is an intermediate product of tantalum chain, stemming from the mining and beneficiation activities of tantalite ore. Tantalum concentrate is the primary input used for the production of pure tantalum products (purity higher than 99%).

This is delivering the service of 'packing, clay product'. It represents a process with an annual production volume of 1,000,000 kg/year and a density of 1,600 m3/kg, while the estimated lifetime of the machines is assumed to be 25 years. The process includes building materials, packing process, loading of the bags on pallets, electricity, waste and machinery. However, the service excludes the input of clay and it should be used alongside 1 kg of it.

molybdenite' is a mineral. It is a black, lead-silvery grey mineral, with very low hardness while it is similar to graphite. It is the direct product from mining and beneficiation and a by-product from copper mine operation and beneficiation. It can be used in the following applications and/or processes: primary ore of molybdenum metal, as lubricant, metal alloys manufacturing like stainless steel and other superalloys, electric devices.

'acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene copolymer', is a plastic product of fossil origin, it is not biodegradable and it is a thermoplastic material. This product consists of 100% virgin material with no content of recycled material. The product is used in the following applications and sectors: weight reduction offers improvement potentials in applications for e.g. automotive parts, household appliances or safety helmets.

'1-propanol' is an organic substance with a CAS no. : 000071-23-8. It is called 'propan-1-ol' under IUPAC naming and its molecular formula is: C3H8O. It is liquid under normal conditions of temperature and pressure and is colourless. It is modelled as a pure substance. Modelling this substance in a solution requires the user to add the solvent of their choice in their models. On a consumer level, is used in the following products: lubricants and greases, anti-freeze products and coating products. On industrial sites, the substance is used for the manufacture of products in the following sectors: laboratory chemicals, coating products, washing & cleaning products and pharmaceuticals. This substance is used for the synthesis of other substances (use as intermediate).

enzymes' refer to the organic substance group of enzymes, represented by a mix of three enzymes. Enzymes are proteins that act as biological catalysts which can accelerate reactions. Enzymes are mostly liquid under normal conditions of temperature and pressure. The product is modelled as a mixture of pure substances. Modelling this substance in a solution requires the user to add the solvent of their choice in their models. On a consumer level, is used in the following products: Besides their biologic funtions (e.g. in the human metabolism), enzymes are applied in varius industries, as the biofuel industry, brewing industry, cooking industy, dairy industry, food processing, molecular biology, paper industry, personal care industry and the starch industry. There is no publicly available information about the consumption of this substance on industrial sites.

This product is mainly used for water treatment. This includes the use of polyaluminium chloride for treatment of drinking water, wastewater, industrial water and swimming pool water (DE DIETRICH SAS 2012).

Reference(s):
Gendorf (2016) Umwelterklärung 2015, Werk Gendorf Industriepark, www.gendorf.de
DE DIETRICH SAS. 2012. The effective technology for water purification is PolyAluminium Chloride (PAC). The product 'tofu' is a processed food product based on soybeans. Soybeans are soaked in water, pressed and centrifuged to obtain a soy base at 5% protein. After coagulation, the resulting whey and curd is pressed to remove the whey. The curd is then pressed and pasteurized, then cooled and cut into blocks.

activity_name: market for optical brighteners, for paper production
product_information:
The product 'optical brighteners, for paper production' is a fluorescent whitening agents, i.e. a substance that in solution or applied to a substrate absorbs ultraviolet light and reemits this absorbed energy as blue fluorescent light. Thus, these substances compensate for the aesthetically undesirable yellowish cast found in white industrial substrates - e.g. in paper or plastics (Siegrist et al. (2003)). 
A variety of different substances are in used as optical brighteners. The product 'optical brighteners, for paper production' represents a 1:1 mixture of fluorescent whitening agent, DAS1, triazinylaminostilben type and fluorescent whitening agent, distyrylbiphenyl type

activity_name: market for cast iron removed by drilling, conventional
reference_product_name: cast iron removed by drilling, conventional
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'cast iron removed by drilling, conventional'. Drilling uses a rotating cutting tool (the drill bit) to remove material from the work piece and it predominately uses the front end for cutting. It is applied for shapes like circular cavity or holes. The process is conventional, meaning that it is controlled manually and needs constant supervision. This dataset represents the drilling of 1 kg of cast iron. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Maintenance and tool (bit) replacements are not included. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

The product "hydraulic lime" represents a binder, which is produced from marl lime or calcareous marl by calcination below the sintering point (temperature of calcination: around 1050ºC, max. 1200ºC). The basic material must have a share of 65 to 75% in calcium carbonate. The calcined hydraulic lime is slaked and milled. To regulate the cementation, gypsum is added during the grinding process. Compared to cement, the SiO2 content of hydraulic lime is varying strongly (5 - 30%). No content of Alit (tricalcium silicate), a high content of Belit (dicalcium silicate) and a higher amount of lime (>5%) are typical for hydraulic lime. Further significant differences to cement are better working properties but worse hydraulic characteristics and lower strength (1 - 5 N/mm2). It is the main raw material for the production of masonry cement or plastering mortar.

activity_name: market for cast iron removed by drilling, computer numerical controlled
reference_product_name: cast iron removed by drilling, computer numerical controlled
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'cast iron removed by drilling, computer numerical controlled'. Drilling uses a rotating cutting tool (the drill bit) to remove material from the work piece and it predominately uses the front end for cutting. It is applied for shapes like circular cavity or holes. The process is conventional, meaning that it is controlled manually and needs constant supervision. The process is computer numerical controlled, meaning that it operates autonomously once programmed. This dataset represents the drilling of 1 kg of cast iron. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Maintenance and tool (bit) replacements are not included. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

The product "pumice" represents 1 kg of pumice stone. Crushed and classified pumice is employed as an additive in light insulating concrete used to make constructional units. Due to its limited compression strength, this building material can only be used for two-storey buildings, or for cladding load-bearing frameworks. Building blocks consist of pure, natural pumice granules treated to remove organic and inorganic im-purities. They are mainly used to build houses. Powdered pumice is used as an abrasive, as a polishing medium, and as an additive to pumice soap. It is also used as an absorption medium due to its large surface area (e.g., in packages carrying breakable liquid containers).

product_information: This is an electric component usually soldered to a printed wiring board (PWB) in electronic circuits. A diode conducts electricity in only one direction. Glass diodes consist of a vacuum tube and two electrodes of which one is a heated cathode and the other a plate electrode. For this product it is assumed that a typical SMD diode in electronic modules has an average weight of 32 mg. This component is designed for surface mounting.

activity_name: market for chlorine, for reuse in titanium tetrachloride production
reference_product_name: chlorine, for reuse in titanium tetrachloride production
product_information: This product represents chlorine produced by the electrolysis of magnesium chloride according to the following reaction: MgCl2 -> Mg + Cl2. The magnesium chloride is obtained during the production of titanium sponge using the Kroll process, in the reduction and distillation step, described by the following reaction: TiCl4 + 2Mg -> Ti + 2MgCl2. Through the electrolysis of magnesium chloride, part of the Mg and Cl is recovered and then reused in the Kroll process. The chlorine is used as input to the chlorination and refining step, which produces titanium tetrachloride according to the following reaction: 2TiO2 + 4Cl2 + 3C -> 2TiCl4 + 2CO + CO2. This product should not be used in any other context.

Potassium chloride sludge is a by product of electrolysis processes from the spent electrolyte. It has a potassium content of 70% as K2O or 58% as potassium (K). It is further used in agriculture as a source of of potassium.

Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, warm, deformation stroke
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, warm, deformation stroke
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, warm, deformation stroke'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. The use of multiple strokes is possible, to produce complex shapes. Strokes deformation is of minor importance when only a single stroke is needed. However, a piece with multiple strokes can significally increase the energy intensity of the process. This dataset represents the stroke deformation of 1 kg steel and it only includes the electricity consumption of the machine during the deformation .

activity_name: market for stamping for thermoplastic composite product
product_information:
This is delivering the service of "stamping for thermoplastic composite product".
Stamping is a process in which a pre-consolidated composite sheet (with thermoplastic resin as PPS, PEEK, PEAK...) is placed under an IR lamp and heated to a temperature above the melting temperature. Once the temperature has been reached, the plate is rapidly transferred (time between 3s and 5s) into the preheated tooling. Then the product is shaped by the pressure of the press. The temperature of the tooling is chosen so that it does not need to be cooled down to demould the part.
1 kg of this process corresponds to 0.78 kg of the final product at the end of the process. The converted amount of material (fibres and resin) is not included into the dataset. The user should list 1 kg of the service and 1 kg of the resin and fibre INPUT for an OUTPUT of 0.78 kg of material processed.
If an activity requires 1 kg of stamped product, the user should list (i) 1/0.78 = 1.28 kg of the service and 1.28 kg of the resin/fibres type processed.

Polyurethane adhesive (PUR adhesive or PUR glue) is a formaldehyde-free adhesive which is used in engineered wood products such as crosslaminated
timber (CLT). Formaldehydes in indoor applications are often associated with concerns of their emissions to ambient air and effects on human health. In PUR adhesive isocyanate is used instead of formaldehyde. The hardening of PUR is induced by the moisture in the wood.
The hardened glue is resistant to moisture and hydrolysis.

Reference(s):
Messmer, Annika & Chaudhary, Abhishek. (2015). Life cycle assessment of adhesives used in wood constructions. Masterthesis, ETH Zürich.

It is a special type of steel, which is used in the core of electromagnetic devices like trasformers, motors, or generator. The functionality of this steel is to allow high electromagnetic permeability, to decrease hysteresis, and low core loss. This is needed to reduce power consumptions and increase efficiency.  This ferro-alloy contains 3.2% of Silicon. The production starts with the hot rolled 3.2%Si coil enter the plant. The coil undergoes the following processes: coil preparation, annealing, picklinkg, cold rolling, degreasing, annealing, decarburisation, box annealing, heat flattening, coating, and finishing. It is sold in form of coils.

The product 'sulfite pulp' is a type of bleached, chemical, wood-containing pulp. It represents Total Chlorine Free (TCF) and Elemental Chlorine Free (ECF) pulp. Chemical pulp is produced by chemical pulping of biomass raw materials, most commonly hardwood and softwood, or also straw from many cereals, bagasse, reed, or esparto grass, and from other annual non-wood plants. The sulfite process is based on calcium, magnesium, and sodium or ammonium bisulfide and produces pulp which is virtually pure cellulose. As the process occurs in acidic conditions one of its drawbacks is that some of the cellulose is hydrolysed, making the sulfite pulp fibres not as strong as other pulps, for example, those obtained with the sulfate process (i.e. kraft pulp). Nonetheless, and although globally the sulfate process is used in up to 90% of chemical pulping processes, sulfite pulps are important for specialty papers (e.g. tissue, glassine) and as a sources of cellulose for various applications within or out of the paper industry.

product_information: This is delivering the service of 'deep drawing, steel, 650 kN press, single stroke'. Deep drawing in the technology of stretching a sheet metal (called blank) into a hollow shape. A punch presses the blank through the die cavity that defines the shape. The service represents the deep drawing of steel with 650 kN press in single stroke mode and includes energy consumption, production infrastructure and machinery, but it excludes the steel input. Degreasing is also not included, so it has to be added if necessary. Deep drawing steel is used when complex geometries with straight sides are required in the end product, like automotive industry.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, hot, deformation stroke
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, hot, deformation stroke
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, hot, deformation stroke'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. The use of multiple strokes is possible, to produce complex shapes. Strokes deformation is of minor importance when only a single stroke is needed. However, a piece with multiple strokes can significally increase the energy intensity of the process. This dataset represents the stroke deformation of 1 kg steel and it only includes the electricity consumption of the machine during the deformation .

product_information: Lightweight concrete blocks are made from aggregates with porous structure, which is mixed with cement and water. In this case, the aggregate is expanded perlite. The raw material mixture (aggregates, cement and water) of lightweight concrete blocks are poured into a mould then squeezed and finaly hardened by air. The main use of lightweight and autoclaved aerated concrete blocks are precision blocks, precision building plates, roof and floor panels, reinforced wall partition blocks, assembly beam, head and pillar units.

activity_name: market for carbon dioxide, in chemical industry
product_information: 'carbon dioxide, in chemical industry' is an inorganic substance with a CAS no. : 000124-38-9. It is called 'carbon dioxide' under IUPAC naming and its molecular formula is: CO2. It is gas under normal conditions of temperature and pressure with no distinct odor. It is modelled as a pure substance. Modelling this substance in a solution requires the user to add the solvent of their choice in their models. On a consumer level, is used in the following products: foods and beverages, fire extinguishers, refrigerants. There is no publicly available information about the consumption of this substance on industrial sites.

It is liquid under normal conditions of temperature and pressure. It is modelled as a pure substance. Modelling this substance in a solution requires the user to add the solvent of their choice in their models. There is no publicly available information about the consumption of this substance on a consumer level. On industrial sites, the substance is used for the manufacture of products in the following sectors: pH regulators and water treatment products and laboratory chemicals.

It can be used in the following applications and/or processes: agricultural chemicals (non-pesticidal), animal feed, catalysts, dyes, intermediates, ion exchange agents, laboratory chemicals, metal products, fuels and related products.

product_information: The service "packing, fibre cement product" represents the packing process for fibre cement products. Data is normalized to a production of 20'000t/a. The lifespan of the infrastructure and the land occupation are assumed to be 50 years. A user employing this module should add the fibre cement product to be packed.

It is solid under normal conditions of temperature and pressure and has a powder form. It is modelled as a pure substance. Modelling this substance in a solution requires the user to add the solvent of their choice in their models. There is no publicly available information about the consumption of this substance on a consumer level. On industrial sites, the substance is used for the manufacture of products in the following sectors: electrolytes for batteries.

This is delivering the service of 'casting, steel, lost-wax'. Lost-wax casting is a process for creating objects, from simple to complex, in a variety of metals (such as gold, silver, brass, or bronze) by casting an original model or pattern. This dataset represents the casting of 1 kg of steel small parts and precision parts with an average mass of 61 kg and the biggest ones weighting 91 kg by lost-wax casting. The service includes steel input, mold manufacturing, melting of stainless steel and alloy inputs, casting, thermal treatment, straightening, cutting, grinding and non-destructive testing. It also includes water and energy consumption, emissions and materials` end-of-life. However, it excludes the final product material and alloying additives. Lost-wax casted steel is used in industries requiring mass produced metal parts with a high level of dimensional accuracy, producing critical components for medical, automotive, aerospace and nuclear energy applications.

It has a higher heating value of 9.56 MJ/kg and a lower heating value of 3.81 MJ/kg .

The product "acrylic filler" represents the most commonly used plaster/mortar based on organic binders. An aqueous solution of acrylic resin is used as binder and the fillers are for example chalk, lime meal, talcum or quartz. Small amounts of pigments, anti foaming agents, softeners, etc. are usually added. Commonly the share of binder is between 7 and 13%. This dataset stands for a 'typical' acrylic filler ready to use (including water).

Marine fish refers to a general category of various fish that are meant for human consumption. They are captured in open sea (hence they are wild) and subsequently landed where they are driven to commercial consumption. Various fishing methods are employed that are documented in the respective producing activities. Marine fish include here, tunas, hake, their by-catches and anchovies. By-catches are all non-target fish species that are captured in the process of fishing another target species. They are not discarded but rather sold in the same market as the target spieces. By-catches usually consitute 4-6% (in terms of mass) of the main catch.
![image](https://db3.ecoinvent.org/images/76f82c8e-9a84-4ad6-b3a0-bd406120787c)

The product 'raw cork' represents the raw bark of cork oak (Quercus suber). Cork is a very light material with a density of 0.12 to 0.2 kg/m3. Cork is further characterized by high elasticity, impermeability, low thermal and electrical conductivity and high resistance to wearing. Cork production in the ecoinvent database represents production in a silvipastoral extensive management system as common in Portugal. Cork oaks are stripped the first time when they are about 25 years old. The so-called 'virgin cork' from the first stripping is of poor quality and thus of minor value. Afterwards, the trees are stripped at intervals of about ten years until the oak is 150 to 200 years old (in Portugal, 140 years is the maximum rotation period in commercial cork production). The harvesting of cork is done manually using special knives and axes. Raw cork is used for the manufacturing of cork stoppers or agglomerates which are used for insulation and flooring materials.
The product 'cauliflower' is a vegetable. It is an annual crop.

activity_name: market for natural stone plate, grounded
product_information: The product "natural stone plate, grounded" represents a grounded granite plate. For the grinding process it has been assumed that about 0.5 mm on each surface of the plate is removed and transferred to dust. The amount of dust per slab is therefore: (0.5 * 0.5 * 2 + 0.5 * 0.03 * 4) * 0.0005 = 0.00028 m3 or 0.77 kg and consequently, the amount of dust per kg of plate is 0.037 kg. It is assumed that the main part of the dust (assumed that about 95%) is bound in the cooling water.

activity_name: market for soybean seed, organic, for sowing
product_information: The product 'soybean seed, organic, for sowing' is a seed. It is an annual crop. This seed product is used for plant propagation. The seed produced at the farm is transported to the processing centre, treated (pre-cleaning, cleaning, eventually drying, chemical dressing (for integrated production) and bag filling), stored and afterwards transported to the regional storage centre. This product is produced through organic agricultural practices which do not include fertiliser and pesticide application.

The weight is expressed as dry mass. Wood chips are produced by chopping larger pieces of wood into chips. Wood chips are used as wood fuel. In the ecoinvent database, wood chips, dry are further used for pulping processes and for the production of engineered wood products, such as fibreboard, and particleboard.

Radio frequency (RF) inductors are used for high resonant frequencies as found in mobile phones or other wireless equipment.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, warm, 4 strokes
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, warm, 4 strokes
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, warm, 4 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes is possible, to produce complex shapes. Warm impact extrusion is performed at temperature between hot and cold impact extrusion, since it is a compromise between the two. This dataset represents warm impact extrusion of 1 kg of steel with 4 stroke. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but it excludes the steel input and the process of degreasing.

product_information: This is delivering the service of 'deep drawing, steel, 10000 kN press, single stroke'. Deep drawing in the technology of stretching a sheet metal (called blank) into a hollow shape. A punch presses the blank through the die cavity that defines the shape. The service represents the deep drawing of steel with 10000 kN press in single stroke mode and includes energy consumption, production infrastructure and machinery, but it excludes the steel input. Degreasing is also not included, so it has to be added if necessary. Deep drawing steel is used when complex geometries with straight sides are required in the end product, like automotive industry.

This is delivering the service of 'sheet rolling, steel'. The activity consists in the cold rolling of steel aiming to produce sheet. For the applications that the products of hot rolling are unsatisfactory, e.g., with respect to cross section, surface quality, dimensional accuracy, and general finish, this service can be used. It is based on cold rolling while the end product has high quality surface finish and precise metallurgical properties for use in high specification purposes. This service is to be used only for un- and low-alloyed steel. The material being rolled is not included in the dataset, thus needs to be added separately. Consequently, for each kg of product rolled, it is necessary to add a kg of base material. he exterior finish of rolled steel is very desirable when aesthetics and visual appeal are a priority. Thus, it used in applications like strips, bars, rods, home appliances, roof and wall systems, metal furniture, aerospace structural members, etc.

The product "calcined clay" represents a pozzolanic material used in cement production. Calcined clay is produced by grinding and heating of natural clay between 500°C and 900°C. This thermal treatment (activation) converts crystalline clay materials to amorphous alumino silicates. When added to cement, amorphous silica reacts with calcium hydroxide formed during calcium silicate hydration from cement, forming C-S-H (hydrated calcium silicate). Secondary reactions may occur due to the reactive alumina content of calcined clay, forming also C-A-H (hydrated calcium aluminate). The addition of calcined clay to cement result in higher sulphate-resistance of the hydrated paste and in low permeability of the cementitious matrix, thus increasing the durability of cementitious products.

The product 'whey' is a by-product of cheese and tofu production. The process step of adding a coagulant (f.e. calcium sulfate) to the soybase (tofu production) or milk (cheese production) results in whey and curds. The curd is then pressed to remove the whey. In cheese production, the whey is separated from the curds through pressing, while in tofu production, the whey is typically discarded as a by-product. Whey contains proteins, lactose, vitamins, and minerals, making it a valuable resource with various potential uses in food and beverage manufacturing, animal feed, and other industries.

The product 'cement, CEM V/B' represents cement (CEM V/B) according to EN 197-1, also known as composite cement. CEM V/B is made of clinker, blast furnace slag 31-50% and 31-50% alternative constituents (weight-%). Alternative constituents may include pozzolan and/or flue ash, besides granulated blast furnace slag.

refuse-derived fuel' is a fossil fuel with a calorific value of 30.79 MJ/kg. It is used as a solid fuel and it is sourced from sorting and drying of combustible wastes, e.g. municipal waste and plastics. It's main applications are in the following sectors: district heating and electricity production.

The product "stucco" represents the natural CaSO4 * ½ H2O, calcinated from gypsum, milled. It is produced by burning of gypsum mineral and milling of stucco. Stucco is used for plaster, moulds, and as a raw material for gypsum boards.

diesel, low-sulfur' is a fossil fuel with a calorific value of 42.8 MJ/kg. It is used as a liquid fuel and is sourced from fractional distillation of crude oil. Its main applications are in the following sectors: automotive, railroad, aviation, agricultural and military vehicle fuel.

This is delivering the service of "filament winding".
Filament winding is a technique used to manufacture hollow, circular, or prismatic parts such as pipes and tanks. It is performed by winding continuous fibre tows onto a rotating mandrel using a specialized winding machine.
1 kg of this process corresponds to 0.94 kg of the final product at the end of the process. The converted amount of material (fibres and resin) is not included into the dataset. The user should list 1 kg of the service and 1 kg of the resin and fibre INPUT for an OUTPUT of 0.94 kg of material processed. If an activity requires 1 kg of wounded product, the user should list (i) 1/0.94 = 1.33 kg of the service and 1.33 kg of the resin/fibres type processed.

activity_name: market for alkylketene dimer sizing agent, for paper production
reference_product_name: alkylketene dimer sizing agent, for paper production
product_information:
The product 'alkylketene dimer sizing agent, for paper production', also called AKD sizer, is a sizing agent used in the paper industry. Sizing agents are used for improving the paper resistance to aqueous liquids. Most often used types of sizing agents are natural products in form of modified natural resins (resin size), starch, or modified starch, as well as synthetic products. One of the most widely used synthetic sizing agent is alkyl ketene dimer (AKD). The main advantage of AKD is that only 0.1% (relative to dry stock) of active substance are needed (European Commission (2000)). 
AKD sizer is synthesized from fatty acids. According to Hubbe (2002) it is most commonly in form of a dispersion - with waxy solid material dispersed as small particles in a solution that contains a stabilizer, i.e. cationic starch.

urea formaldehyde resin', is a plastic product of fossil origin, it is not biodegradable and it is a thermoset material. This product consists of 100% virgin material with no content of recycled material. The product is used in the following applications and sectors:  widely used to cure high MW solid epoxy resins at elevated temperatures (up to 200 °C) for metal can, drum, and coil coatings applications. The resultant coatings have excellent chemical resistance, good mechanical properties, and no effects on taste (adding or taking away taste from packaged foods or drinks). The vast majority of the food and beverage cans produced in theworld today are coated internally with epoxy-formaldehyde resin coatings. The can and coil coatings, generally, are cross-linked with phenol, melamine, or urea-formaldehyde condensation products at elevated temperatures (150-200 °C) with acid catalysts.

activity_name: market for lightweight concrete block, expanded vermiculite
product_information: Lightweight concrete blocks are made from aggregates with porous structure, which is mixed with cement and water. In this case, the aggregate is expanded vermiculite. The raw material mixture (aggregates, cement and water) of lightweight concrete blocks are poured into a mould then squeezed and finaly hardened by air. The main use of lightweight and autoclaved aerated concrete blocks are precision blocks, precision building plates, roof and floor panels, reinforced wall partition blocks, assembly beam, head and pillar units.

Thulium is a lanthanide, a rare earth element, that is predominatly obtained from the ion adsorption clays of southern China - representing approximately 98% of world production - as a by-product of yttrium and gadolinium extraction. Thulium oxide (Tm2O3) is used in a number of different applications, most prominently as an active laser medium material and in x-ray devices.

The product "gravel, crushed" represents crushed stone from open granite or basalt quarries. This is delivering the service of "hand lay-up".
hand lay-up is a process in which composite materials (resin and fibres) are placed in an open mould, where they cure or harden while exposed to the air.
1 kg of this process corresponds to 0.87 kg of the final product at the end of the process. The converted amount of material (fibres and resin) is not included into the dataset. The user should list 1 kg of the service and 1 kg of the resin and fibre INPUT for an OUTPUT of 0.87 kg of material processed.
If an activity requires 1 kg of open contact moulded product, the user should list (i) 1/0.87= 1.15 kg of the service and 1.15 kg of the resin/fibres type processed.

activity_name: market for lightweight concrete block, expanded clay
product_information: Lightweight concrete blocks are made from aggregates with porous structure, which is mixed with cement and water. In this case, the aggregate is expanded clay. The raw material mixture (aggregates, cement and water) of lightweight concrete blocks are poured into a mould then squeezed and finaly hardened by air. The main use of lightweight and autoclaved aerated concrete blocks are precision blocks, precision building plates, roof and floor panels, reinforced wall partition blocks, assembly beam, head and pillar units.

This product consists of 100% virgin material with no content of recycled material. The product is used in the following applications and sectors: coatings, adhesives, manufacturing of large glass fiber reinforced plastic articles like sanitary-ware, tanks, pipes, gratings, and high performance components for the marine and transportation industry such as closure panels, body panels, fenders, boat hulls and decks.

activity_name: market for rye grain, feed, organic
product_information: The product 'rye grain, feed, organic' represents 1 kg processed feedstuff (fresh weight). Rye grain is an annual crop. This product is used for animal feed. Its production includes the feedstuff processing (crushing or milling, heat treatment, dosing, mixing squeezing and pelleting), storage of the feed mixes, water use, wastewater treatment, transformation and use of land related to the storage buildings. This product is produced through organic agricultural practices which do not include fertiliser and pesticide application.

The product "limestone, unprocessed" represents a naturally occurring mineral that consists principally of calcium carbonate (CaCO3). It is found in many forms and is classified in terms of its origin, chemical composition, structure, and geological formation. This product is modelled as 97% calcite (CaCO3), 2% silica (SiO2) and 1% magnesite (MgCO3). Limestone is generally used for construction, cement, quicklime, agriculture, metal refining and flue gas desulphurisation. In construction, limestone is the most widely used crushed rock, although it is generally sold in form of sand and gravel.

activity_name: market for ground granulated blast furnace slag
product_information: The product "ground granulated blast furnace slag" represents the treatment of molten blast furnace slag, producing an output of ground granulated blast furnace slag (GGBFS, also referred to as slag cement). The ground granulated blast furnace slag (GGBFS) is a by-product of iron manufacturing which when added to concrete improves its properties such as workability, strength and durability. This material is obtained by the heating of iron ore, limestone and coke at a temperature about 1500 degree Celsius.

activity_name: market for basic oxygen furnace sludge, for recovery
reference_product_name: basic oxygen furnace sludge, for recovery
product_information: Sludges are produced during wet scrubbing of flue gases produced during steelmaking. They comprise, in principle, iron and other metal oxides, but composition varies widely based on technology level, charge composition, and the types and quantities of additives that are used. Basic oxygen furnace (BOF) sludges may be sent for internal or external recovery or disposal. This is BOF sludge that is sent for internal or external recovery.

The product 'corrugated board box' represents a box made of corrugated board. Corrugated board consist of different types of containerboards, namely linerboard and fluting medium. Linerboard is a flat layer of board, which can be further distinguished in kraftliner and testliner, depending on whether it is of virgin or recycled origin, respectively. Fluting medium is the corrugated middle layer between a top and bottom layer of linerboard. Together all three layers constitute corrugated board. To produce corrugated board boxes, the corrugated board is cut into sheets, printed, slotted, folded, and glued. Corrugated board boxes are used for the packaging of products for protection and transportation.

reference_product_name: single use paper wrap, for food packaging, from virgin fibre
product_information: The product 'single use paper wrap, for food packaging, from virgin fibre' is a type of paper-based tableware for fast food applications. It represents wraps for serving of burgers and fry bags for serving of fries. The wrap is made of virgin fibres and it is bleached and oil-resistant. The production process involves multiple stages such as preparing virgin fibres, pulping, bleaching, papermaking, followed by a converting process that includes printing, coating, cutting and folding of the final tableware items. The tableware is intended for single-use applications in fast-food establishments, commonly known as Quick Service Restaurants (QSRs).

The product 'sulfate pulp, bleached', also called unbleached kraft pulp, is a type of unbleached, chemical, wood-containing pulp.  It represents sulfate pulp obtained from softwood. Softwood pulp is made from coniferous trees, most typically from different species of pine and spruce. Sulfate pulp fibres from softwood fibers are long and slender and are used to give the paper and board products their strength properties. The pulp, or fibres from the wood are separated in a kraft cooking process in a so-called fibre line. In a recovery line, the chemicals used in the cooking process are recovered to be used again and energy is produced. The third process is effluent treatment where gaseous and liquid discharges to the environment are continuously minimized and monitored.Unbleached sulfate pulp is commonly used in liner for corrugated board, wrappings, sack and bag papers, envelopes and other unbleached specialty papers.

activity_name: market for aluminium removed by turning, average, conventional
reference_product_name: aluminium removed by turning, average, conventional
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'aluminium removed by turning, average, conventional'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. The process is conventional, meaning that it is controlled manually and needs constant supervision. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of aluminium. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

Pultrusion is a method of manufacturing that refers to a continuous process that utilizes fibre reinforcements and resin matrices to produce constant cross-sectional profiles.
1 kg of this process corresponds to 0.94 kg of the final product at the end of the process. The converted amount of material (fibres and resin) is not included into the dataset. The user should list 1 kg of the service and 1 kg of the resin and fibre INPUT for an OUTPUT of 0.94 kg of material processed.
If an activity requires 1 kg of infused product, the user should list (i) 1/0.94 = 1.06 kg of the service and 1.06 kg of the resin/fibres type processed.

The product "activated bentonite" represents a mix of products, put together from the production processes of alkaline activated bentonite (38%), acid activated bentonite (61%) and catalytic converter (1%). product_information:
This product represents magnesium chloride that is produced during the production of titanium sponge using the Kroll process. The reduction and distillation step consists of the following reaction: TiCl4 + 2Mg -> Ti + 2MgCl2. 
The magnesium chloride produced from this reaction is sent to an electrolysis process, which recovers part of the magnesium and chlorine. This product should not be used in any other context.

Modelling this substance in a solution requires the user to add the solvent of their choice in their models. Bromoxynil is a herbicide applied to control broad-leaved weeds. It is used post-emergence e.g. on cereals, maize, sorghum, onions, garlic, mint and turf. Bromoxynil inhibits the photosynthetic electron transfer and the oxidative phosphorylation in the mitochondria. This  exacerbates the energy production and respiration of plants. The product 'chemi-thermomechanical pulp' (CTMP) is a type of unbleached, mechanical, wood-containing pulp. Mechanical pulp is weaker than chemical pulp (sulfate and sulfite pulp) but has a higher light-scattering coefficients and opacity. In the CTMP production process, wood chips are impregnated with chemicals (sulfonation with Na2SO3) before they are mechanically defibrated in the refiner under high pressure (~about 3 bar) or at atmospheric pressure, mainly in two stages. During mechanical defibration, lignin is plasticised and remains in the pulp. CTMP cannot be brightened effectively by oxidative and reductive bleaching. Most of the mechanical pulp is produced at integrated pulp mills which directly process it further into (paper-) products and only a minor part of pulp production is sold on the market. CTMP  is mainly used for Printing & writing papers, tissue and packaging boards.
The product 'coriander' is a herb. It is an annual crop.

This is delivering the service of 'calendering, rigid sheets'. Calendering is a speciality process for high-volume, high quality plastic film and sheet, mainly used for PVC as well as for certain other modified thermoplastics. The melted polymer is subject to heat and pressure in an extruder and formed into sheet or film by calendering rolls. The temperature and speed of the rolls influences the properties of the film. Calendering allows speciality surface treatments of the film or sheet such as embossing or enhancing the physical properties or in-line lamination. In the service of calendaring, 1 kg of this process corresponds to 0.997 kg of calendered rigid plastic sheets. The converted amount of plastics is not included into the dataset. This service should be used along with 1 kg of thermoplastic material, usually PVC.

activity_name: market for uranium, enriched 3.9%, in fuel element for light water reactor
reference_product_name: uranium, enriched 3.9%, in fuel element for light water reactor
product_information: 'uranium, enriched 3.9%, in fuel element for light water reactor' represents the enriching of uranium dioxide fuel for light water nuclear reactors (PWR and BWR), with no MOX (mixed oxides), no RepU (reprocessed fuel) and no HEU (highly enriched uranium from warheads) fuel elements.

The product 'sulfate pulp, bleached', also called bleached kraft pulp, is a type of bleached, chemical, wood-containing pulp.  It represents Elemental Chlorine Free (ECF) and Totally Chlorine Free (TCF) kraft pulp. The product bleached sulfate pulp represents a mix of softwood pulp, hardwood pulp and eucalyptus pulp. Softwood pulp is made from coniferous trees, most typically from different species of pine and spruce. Hardwood pulp is made from non-coniferous trees, in Nordic countries primarily of birch. Eucalyptus hardwood pulp is made exclusively of eucalyptus species. Sulfate pulp fibres from softwood fibres are long and slender and are used to give the paper and board products their strength properties. Sulfate pulp fibres from hardwood short and can be rounder and their number per gram of pulp is much larger; they give the end product its smoothness, softness and optical properties like opacity. The pulp, or fibres from the wood are separated in a kraft cooking process in a so-called fibre line. In a recovery line, the chemicals used in the cooking process are recovered to be used again and energy is produced. The third process is effluent treatment where gaseous and liquid discharges to the environment are continuously minimized and monitored. Bleached sulfate pulp is used for the production of tissue (toilet paper), graphic papers , specialty products, packaging, fluff pulp, viscose, and CMC.

reference_product_name: deep drawing, steel, 38000 kN press, single stroke
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'deep drawing, steel, 38000 kN press, single stroke'. Deep drawing in the technology of stretching a sheet metal (called blank) into a hollow shape. A punch presses the blank through the die cavity that defines the shape. The service represents the deep drawing of steel with 38000 kN press in single stroke mode and includes energy consumption, production infrastructure and machinery, but it excludes the steel input. Degreasing is also not included, so it has to be added if necessary. Deep drawing steel is used when complex geometries with straight sides are required in the end product, like automotive industry.

The product "cement mortar" represents a mixture of cement and sand. This product belongs to mortar group III, which means that the ratio of the ingredients can vary in terms of cement/sand volume ratio from 1:2.5 to 1:7 depending on the scope of the cement mortar. Cement mortar can be used indoor as well as outdoor. It is suitable for walling, plastering and mending of parts. It is also used to make socles and walls underground or for plastering areas which have to be highly shock-resistant. The differences between mortar and plaster lie in the capacity of plasters to take better finish, which depend to a very large extent on the type of sand used in the mix. For mortar we use coarse particles of sand.

Modelling this substance in a solution requires the user to add the solvent of their choice in their models.1-methylcyclopropene is a plant growth regulator which is applied comercially postharvest to slow down the ripening process of fruit and help maintain the freshness of ornamental plants and flowers. activity_name: market for autoclaved aerated concrete block
product_information: The product "autoclaved aerated concrete block" represents hardened blocks consisting mainly of calcium silicate hydrate and air voids. The raw materials for autoclaved aerated concrete blocks are silica sand and binding agents (milled quicklime, cement and anhydrite) which are mixed with water to a suspension. Aluminium powder is added and the mixture is poured into a mould. The aluminium powder is reacting with the alcaline and siliceous additives and forms, by hydrogen release, pores with a diameter between 2 and 3mm. Hardening is assumed to be by air-drying. The main use of lightweight and autoclaved aerated concrete blocks are precision blocks, precision building plates, roof and floor panels, reinforced wall partition blocks, assembly beam, head and pillar units.

product_information: This is delivering the service of 'deep drawing, steel, 3500 kN press, single stroke'. Deep drawing in the technology of stretching a sheet metal (called blank) into a hollow shape. A punch presses the blank through the die cavity that defines the shape. The service represents the deep drawing of steel with 3500 kN press in single stroke mode and includes energy consumption, production infrastructure and machinery, but it excludes the steel input. Degreasing is also not included, so it has to be added if necessary. Deep drawing steel is used when complex geometries with straight sides are required in the end product, like automotive industry.

The service "tempering, flat glass" represents the toughening of 1 kg of glass, a special process of solidification of a glass sheet in order to make it particularly resistant to breakages. The glass sheet is heated to a temperature just below its softening point (about 640°C, thermal tempering) and then immediately cooled by special jets of cold-air. These harden the surface of the glass, giving the inside more time to cool. This allows the external layer to crystallize into a wider lattice while the inside solidifies with greater compression than in the crystal lattice. The most common application is automotive glass. A user employing this module should add the respective amount of raw glass, as the throughput of raw glass is not included.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, warm, initial warming

product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, warm, initial warming'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. Initial warming is a part of the warm impact extrusion, including the warming of the metal before it is deformed. This dataset represents the warming of 1 kg of steel and it is considered as an integral part of the warm impact extrusion dataset. It only includes the energy consumption for the warming of the steel.

CEM IV/B is a blended cement that is made by partially replacing 36-55% of Portland cement with fly ash, which is a waste from power stations, and pozzolana (weight-%). Natural pozzolanas include volcanic ashes, opaline shales, and cherts that contain reactive compounds of silicon and/or aluminium. Fly ash is a synthetic pozzolana.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of aluminium, 1 stroke
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of aluminium, 1 stroke'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes it is possible to produce complex shapes. This dataset represents the impact extrusion of 1 kg of aluminium with 1 stroke. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but is excludes the input of alumimium and the process of degreasing is not included.

activity_name: market for glass fibre reinforced plastic, polyamide, injection moulded
reference_product_name: glass fibre reinforced plastic, polyamide, injection moulded
product_information: The product "glass fibre reinforced plastic, polyamide, injection moulded" is produced by injection moulding of glass fibre with polyamide resin. This product is used to replace steel, wood, cardboard, etc. in high performance applications: electricity (optical cable or central strength members) and electronics (printed circuit boards), energy (windmills, solar collectors), building and infrastructure (building façade, bridges, road pavement). Also, consumer goods (casing for household appliances and computers) and transportation (car body panels, truck cabins) are made with glass fibre reinforced plastic.

The product battery, Na-NiCl, rechargeable is a Na-NiCl battery made with Na-NiCl cells. A battery is a device that stores chemical energy and converts it to electrical energy. It is made of modules in which cells are packed together and connected through cables. It can be used in the following applications and/or processes: grid energy storage/regulation, industrial applications, electric vehicles

The product 'stone groundwood pulp' (SGW) is a type of bleached, mechanical, wood-containing pulp. Mechanical pulp is weaker than chemical pulp (sulfate and sulfite pulp) but has a higher light-scattering coefficients and opacity. For the production of SGW pulp, wood logs are pressed against a rotating grinder stone with simultaneous addition of water. The majority of mechanical pulp is produced at integrated pulp mills which directly process it further into (paper-) products and only a minor part of pulp production is sold on the market. SGW pulp is mainly used for Printing & writing papers and newsprint.

The product 'paper sack", also called cement sack, paper bag, and pet food sack, represents an average European paper sack, i.e. is a hybrid mix of all types of multiwall paper sacks in Europe. It represents a mix of paper sacks composed of brown (unbleached) and white (bleached) kraft paper, LDPE and HDPE plastic liners and different kinds of glues. Paper sacks are made of sack kraft paper which is a porous kraft paper with high elasticity and high tear resistance, designed for packaging products with high demands for strength and durability. Paper sacks are used for a variety of packaging purposes with high demands for strength and durability, e.g. cement sacks and pet food sacks. It should be handled with care when used for sacks without plastic liners or small bags such as carrier bags.

activity_name: market for cast iron removed by milling, dressing
reference_product_name: cast iron removed by milling, dressing
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'cast iron removed by milling, dressing'. Milling is a chipping process where the piece of the material is fixed to a movable table while the cutting energy is provided by the rotating cutting tool. It is used for complex shaping and it can be applied to metals, wood and plastic materials. When the process is performed with dressing operation, then only minor amounts of materials are removed to produce the exact dimensions and smooth surface. This dataset represents the milling of 1kg of cast iron. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, energy consumption, water consumption and materials end-of-life. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

The product 'cement, CEM II/B' represents the production mix of CEM II/B-M (T-LL), CEM II/B-LL, CEM II/B-M (V-LL) and CEM II/B-T according to EN 197-1. CEM II/B is made of clinker and 21-35% alternative constituents (weight-%). Alternative constituents include limestone, granulated blast furnace slag, silica sand (max. 10%), pozzolan, flue ash or burned slate.

activity_name: market for brass removed by turning, primarily dressing, computer numerical controlled
reference_product_name: brass removed by turning, primarily dressing, computer numerical controlled
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'brass removed by turning, primarily dressing, computer numerical controlled'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. When the process is performed with dressing operation, then only minor amounts of materials are removed to produce the exact dimensions and smooth surface. The process is computer numerical controlled, meaning that it operates autonomously once programmed. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of brass. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

melamine formaldehyde resin', is a plastic product of fossil origin, it is not biodegradable and it is a thermoset material. This product consists of 100% virgin material with no content of recycled material. The product is used in the following applications and sectors:  widely used to cure high molecular weight solid epoxy resins at elevated temperatures (up to 200 °C) for metal can, drum, and coil coatings applications. The resultant coatings have excellent chemical resistance, good mechanical properties, and no effects on taste (adding or taking away taste from packaged foods or drinks). The vast majority of the food and beverage cans produced in theworld today are coated internally with epoxy-formaldehyde resin coatings. The can and coil coatings, generally, are crosslinked with phenol, melamine, or urea-formaldehyde condensation products at elevated temperatures (150-200 °C) with acid catalysts.

activity_name: market for compression of sheet moulding compound
product_information:
This is delivering the service of "compression of sheet moulding compound".
Compression SMC includes SMC prepregs manufacturing and compression moulding of SMC. SMC prepregs are laminates composed of resin (polyester), cut fibres (glass or carbon) and fillers in sheet form.
The method by which SMC is produced consists of applying the resin in the form of a paste to a film; next, the fibres are cut and added to the paste. the mixture is passed through a calendering system, which ensures a uniform thickness. Finally, the sheets produced, are stored in rolls. SMC prepregs are shaped to their final form by compression which consists of placing SMC sheets in a heated mould and applying products are obtained Layers of SMC are placed in the lower half of a heated mould. When the tool is closed, the material fills the cavity and polymerizes. Once fully cured, the parts are released and left to cool down.
1 kg of this process corresponds to 0.93 kg of the final product at the end of the process. The converted amount of material (fibres and resin) is not included into the dataset.
The user should list 1 kg of the service and 1kg of the resin and fibre INPUT for an OUTPUT of 0.93 kg of material processed.
If an activity requires 1 kg of product, the user should list (i) 1/0.93 = 1.08 kg of the service and 1.08 kg of the resin/fibres type processed.

The product 'containerboard, fluting medium' is a specific type of board. Containerboard is the umbrella term for linerboard and fluting medium, which are both required to produce corrugated board. Linerboard is a flat layer of board, which can be further distinguished in kraftliner and testliner, depending on whether it is of virgin or recycled origin, respectively. Fluting medium is the corrugated middle layer between a top and bottom layer of linerboard. Together all three layers constitute corrugated board. This product is a mix of virgin fluting medium (commonly called semichemical fluting) and recycled fluting (wellenstoff).

The product "clinker" represents Portland cement clinker, also known as cement clinker or just clinker. Crystallised calcium silicates that are rich in calcium oxide and can rapidly react with the mixing water make up at least two-thirds of the commercial portland cement clinker. The cement industry uses limestone, marl and clay for producing clinker. Therefore the raw material mixture is calcined in a rotary kiln. Clinker is then used as an important component for the production of cement. The difference between clinker and quicklime used for products in the binding agent industry is mainly the calcination temperature which is a maximum of 1450ºC for clinker and a maximum of 1200ºC for quicklime.

The product "limestone, crushed, washed" represents 1 kg of washed crushed limestone. Limestone is a naturally occurring mineral that consists principally of calcium carbonate (CaCO3). It is found in many forms and is classified in terms of its origin, chemical composition, structure, and geological formation. This product is modelled as 97% calcite (CaCO3), 2% silica (SiO2) and 1% magnesite (MgCO3). Limestone occurs widely throughout the world and is an essential raw material for many industries. Limestone is generally used for construction, cement, quicklime, agriculture, metal refining and flue gas desulphurisation: Construction: Limestone is the most widely used crushed rock, although it is generally sold in form of sand and gravel. Cement: The production of cement is the main use for chalk, and a major use for dense, high-calcium limestone. Quicklime: Approximately 1.8 t of limestone is required per 1.0 t of quicklime. Agriculture: Many crops grow best under neutral to slightly acid conditions (i.e., pH between 6 and 7). Thus soils which are more acidic than pH 6 generally benefit from the application of limestone. Metal refining: When limestone is used in metal refining it is initially converted to quicklime, which reacts with acidic oxides (e.g., SiO2, Al2O, and Fe2O3) to produce molten slag. Flue gas desulphurisation: A growing use of limestone is in the treatment of flue gas from combustion processes to remove sulphur dioxide.

activity_name: market for paper, woodcontaining, lightweight coated
product_information: The product "paper, wood containing, lightweight coated", also called LWC paper, is a type of graphic paper. More specific, it is a coated, mechanical paper which means that less than 90% of the fibres are in form of chemical pulp. Coated means that this paper is on one or even both sides coated with a coating mineral like kaolin or calcium carbonate. Usually, this kind of paper mills contain an integrated mechanical pulp production and sometimes also an integrated deinking equipment for recovered paper. The used sulfate pulp is usually produced outside of the paper mill. This paper grade is predominantly used for print materials such as magazines and journals that require a higher paper quality than newsprint paper.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, warm, 2 strokes
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, warm, 2 strokes
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, warm, 2 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes is possible, to produce complex shapes. Warm impact extrusion is performed at temperature between hot and cold impact extrusion, since it is a compromise between the two. This dataset represents warm impact extrusion of 1 kg of steel with 2 stroke. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but it excludes the steel input and the process of degreasing.

The product "expanded perlite" represents perlite expanded by shock heating at a temperature of 870 - 1090 °C. The volume increase is 15 - 30 fold. Density of the expanded perlite is 60 - 300kg/m3. The most important uses of expanded perlite (normally treated to render it hydrophobic) include filled plaster board and wall elements, insulating silicate-bonded boards for heat and flame protection, insulating bitumen-bonded boards for back facing, insulating boards and coating materials with poly-mer binders, mortar (bonded with cement, plaster, or silicate), polymer dispersion mortars and plasters for thermal insulation and prevention of condensation on walls and ceilings (e.g., in swimming pools, high humidity rooms).

Kaolin is a clay mineral, more precisely an aluminium hydroxide silicate which corresponds to the chemical formula Al2O3.2SiO2.2H2O. It is a naturally occurring weathered product of granite, sold for its whiteness, purity and physical and chemical properties. In the United States, about 70% of all kaolin produced by the wet process are used as filler or coating in the paper industry. Other industries using this mineral are, for example, the ceramics industry (for whiteware, sanitary ware, insulators, refractories), the paints and inks producers (extender pigment) or the rubber and plastics industry (functional filler material).

This is delivering the service of 'casting, aluminium, lost-wax'. Lost-wax casting is a process for creating objects, from simple to complex, for a variety of metals (such as gold, silver, brass, or bronze) by casting an original model or pattern. This dataset represents the casting of 1 kg of aluminium and aluminum low-alloy parts by lost-wax casting. The service includes the input of aluminium ingots and alloys, along with their melting, casting, dewaxing, cutting, grinding, straightening, machining, and non-destructive testing. It also includes water and energy consumption, emissions and materials` end-of-life. However, it excludes the final product material and alloying additives. Lost-wax casted aluminium is used in industries requiring mass produced metal parts with a high level of dimensional accuracy, producing critical components for aerospace, automotive, and medical applications.

activity_name: market for brass removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled
reference_product_name: brass removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'brass removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. When the process is performed with roughing operation, then large amounts of materials are removed at once. The process is computer numerical controlled, meaning that it operates autonomously once programmed. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of brass. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

activity_name: market for retention aid, for paper production
product_information: The product 'retention aid, for paper production' is an auxiliary material used mainly to maintain an adequate efficiency, drainage, and cleanliness of a paper machines (Hubbe (2002)). Retention aids reduce the loss of raw materials and increase the production of paper. A further effect is the possibility to retain additives (incl. Fillers). Therefore, most often when speaking about 'retention aids', cationic or anionic acrylamine copolymers are mentioned. But besides them, a variety of further materials can be used instead (e.g. bentonite, colloidal silica, aluminium sulphate, polyethylene oxide)

Alkyl sulfates (C12-14) are nonionic surfactants. This type of product can either have no colour or have a colour that is in between yellow and brown. The main application of surfactants is in the production of cleaning products for both household application and personal care (Kosswig 2000). Alkyl sulphates are used usually in a concentration around 4%. The maximum concentration used is 20%. Alkyl sulphates can and are usually mixed with other surfactants (OECD 2007).
 This substances can either be produced in solid or liquid from and are usually sold with a purity range between 30 and 95% (OECD 2007). It is modelled as a pure substance. Modelling this substance in a solution requires the user to add the solvent of their choice in their models.
 
 Reference(s):
 Kosswig, K. 2000. Surfactants. In Ullmann's Encyclopedia of Industrial Chemistry, Electronic Release, Vol.35, pp.431-505. Wiley-VCH, Weinheim.
 OECD. 2007. SIDS Initial Assessment Report For SIAM 25. Alkyl Sulfates, Alkane Sulfonates and -Olefin Sulfonates. Retrieved from: http://webnet.oecd.org/Hpv/ui/handler.axd?id=dbf0a76c-236d-4fdc-b4d4-4f4634778dff, accessed 1 March 2017

Coconut oil is an oleochemical raw material used in the detergent  surfactant  production. The crude oil is further refined to become edible or used as it is to produce biodiesel.

The products are manual dishwashing detergents, window glass trigger spray cleaners, bathroom trigger spray cleaners, acid toilet cleaners and bleach toilet cleaners. activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, warm, 3 strokes
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, warm, 3 strokes
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, warm, 3 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes is possible, to produce complex shapes. Warm impact extrusion is performed at temperature between hot and cold impact extrusion, since it is a compromise between the two. This dataset represents warm impact extrusion of 1 kg of steel with 3 stroke. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but it excludes the steel input and the process of degreasing.

The dataset was modelled from the arithmetic mean of all inputs and outputs of the production of several representing substances. The reference product is modelled to represent a pure substance. product_information: The product 'palm kernel oil, crude' is produced by means of wet milling in a oil mill producing palm oil, palm kernel oil and palm kernel meal. activity_name: market for rosin size, for paper production
product_information: The product 'rosin size, for paper production' is a sizing agent used in the paper industry. Sizing agents are used for improving the paper resistance to aqueous liquids. Rosin size is produced by treating rosin with an alkali. Rosin is a residue derived from the distillation and subsequent removal of volatile materials (notably turpentine) of the gum of pines. Rosin is insoluble in water and therefore is treated by saponification with caustic soda or soda ash. For paper production, aluminium sulphate is usually added to the rosin which leads to the precipitation of the size (Roberts, M. T., and Etherington D. (1998))
