activity_name: market for aluminium removed by turning, average, computer numerical controlled
reference_product_name: aluminium removed by turning, average, computer numerical controlled
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'aluminium removed by turning, average, computer numerical controlled'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. The process is computer numerical controlled, meaning that it operates autonomously once programmed. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of aluminium. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

activity_name: market for transistor, wired, small size, through-hole mounting
reference_product_name: transistor, wired, small size, through-hole mounting
product_information: This is an electric component usually soldered to a printed wiring board (PWB) in electronic circuits. Transistors are made of semiconductor material and are used to switch or amplify electric signals. For this product it is assumed that a wired, small-size transistor in electronic modules has an average weight of 0.818 g. This component is designed for through-hole mounting.

activity_name: market for titania slag, 85% titanium dioxide
product_information:
Titania slag, which comes from ilmenite, contains up to 85 per cent titanium dioxide, while rutile contains up to 94 per cent titanium dioxide. The largest application of titanium dioxide is as a white pigment representing between 95 and 98 per cent of worldwide TiO2 consumption. The remaining uses are as a flux for electric welding rods and in certain metallurgical and electronic applications. 
Titanium dioxide is beneficiated by end-users to provide brilliance and opacity to products such as paints, plastics or paper.  Manufacturers mainly use rutile in welding rod fluxes	as	it	stabilises	the	arc	and	protects	the	molten metal from oxidation - forming a superior welding joint. In its titanium metal form, it is used extensively in the aerospace and aviation industries because of its lightness, strength and corrosion – and heat-resistance. These properties also make it ideal	for	use	in	artificial	hip	joints,	pacemakers	and	spectacle frames.

The product 'clay roof tile' represents 1 kg of roof tile made from clay. Its production includes the first grinding process, wet process (second grinding, mixing and plastifying), storage, forming and cutting, drying, firing, loading and storage. Clay roof tiles are impermeable.

activity_name: market for aluminium removed by milling, dressing
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'aluminium removed by milling, dressing'. Milling is a chipping process where the piece of the material is fixed to a movable table while the cutting energy is provided by the rotating cutting tool. It is used for complex shaping and it can be applied to metals, wood and plastic materials. When the process is performed with dressing operation, then only minor amounts of materials are removed to produce the exact dimensions and smooth surface. This dataset represents the milling of 1kg of aluminium. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, energy consumption, water consumption and materials end-of-life. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

activity_name: market for aluminium removed by drilling, computer numerical controlled
reference_product_name: aluminium removed by drilling, computer numerical controlled
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'aluminium removed by drilling, computer numerical controlled'. Drilling uses a rotating cutting tool (the drill bit) to remove material from the work piece and it predominately uses the front end for cutting. It is applied for shapes like circular cavity or holes. The process is conventional, meaning that it is controlled manually and needs constant supervision. The process is computer numerical controlled, meaning that it operates autonomously once programmed. This dataset represents the drilling of 1 kg of aluminium. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Maintenance and tool (bit) replacements are not included. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.
The product 'maize silage' represents preserved pasture used for animal feed (fresh matter). Maize is an annual crop. The maize is cut, collected and then fermented.

Hafnium sponge is the product of the Kroll process, during which the HfCl4 obtained from the zirconium / hafnium separation process is reduced by adding magnesium to hafnium sponge and magnesium chloride. Hafnium is mostly used in the atomic energy industry as a neutron absorber or for the production of nuclear reaction control rods and protection devices. Other applications include the use as cathode in X-ray tube or as an electronic material for the manufacture of photovoltaics.

activity_name: market for limestone, crushed, for mill
product_information: The product "limestone, crushed, for mill" represents 1 kg of crushed limestone, prepared to be milled. Limestone is a naturally occurring mineral that consists principally of calcium carbonate (CaCO3). It is found in many forms and is classified in terms of its origin, chemical composition, structure, and geological formation. This product is modelled as 97% calcite (CaCO3), 2% silica (SiO2) and 1% magnesite (MgCO3). Limestone occurs widely throughout the world and is an essential raw material for many industries. Limestone is generally used for construction, cement, quicklime, agriculture, metal refining and flue gas desulphurisation: Construction: Limestone is the most widely used crushed rock, although it is generally sold in form of sand and gravel. Cement: The production of cement is the main use for chalk, and a major use for dense, high-calcium limestone. Quicklime: Approximately 1.8 t of limestone is required per 1.0 t of quicklime. Agriculture: Many crops grow best under neutral to slightly acid conditions (i.e., pH between 6 and 7). Thus soils which are more acidic than pH 6 generally benefit from the application of limestone. Metal refining: When limestone is used in metal refining it is initially converted to quicklime, which reacts with acidic oxides (e.g., SiO2, Al2O, and Fe2O3) to produce molten slag. Flue gas desulphurisation: A growing use of limestone is in the treatment of flue gas from combustion processes to remove sulphur dioxide.

This item represents "solar glass, low-iron", or water white crystal. It is a colourless glass produced from low-iron silica sand and low-iron limestone. It is used in the manufacturing of solar collectors and photovoltaic panels.

Reformate (gasoline) is the main output of a (catalytic) reforming process and an educt for either petrochemicals   or gasoline with a high octane rating (95-100). It is a mixture of different products (e.g. toluol , cyclohexane or methylcyclohexane) and has an average of 30-50% paraffines, 45-60% aromates, 0-2% olefines, 0-3% naphthenes.

sodium borates' is a product that belongs to the metal sector. It is soft, silvery-white, highly reactive and alkali metal. It represents a mix of borax concentrates, with a content of 0.12824 kg of boron. It can be used in the following applications and/or processes: mainly in the glass industry, also as household cleaner and a booster for laundry detergent.

The product 'paper, woodfree, coated", also called coated fine paper, is a type of graphic paper. Wood free paper is defined as containing at least 90% of the fibres in form of chemical pulp. Coated means that this paper is on one or even both sides coated with a coating mineral like kaolin or calcium carbonate. Woodfree papers have a small tendency to yellow and are characterized by high strength. This product represents a mix of coated fined paper produced at integrated and non-integrated mills. Coated woodfree paper is used for all kind of printing and other graphic purposes.

product_information: Dusts that are produced during steelmaking in a blast furnace (BF) after separation from flue gases. BF gas dust may be sent for internal or external recovery or disposal. This represents high grade material that is sent for recovery.

Folding Box Board FBB (also known as GC and UC) is a packaging grade of board used to manufacture cartons. It is typically made using predominantly virgin fibres, but may incoroprate some recovered fibres. This grade is typically made of mechanical pulp sandwiched between two layers of chemical pulp with up to three layers of coating on the top or printing surface and one layer of coating or sizing on the reverse. The manufacture of Folding Box Board, FBB, is  to a large degree integrated but usually the required chemical pulp is purchased.  Typical uses include pharmaceuticals, confectionery, frozen and chilled foods. The board is converted into a carton at a converting site which typically includes a printing process and cutting/creasing of the board into carton blanks, ready for delivery to and erection by the packer/filler.

Supplementary cementitious materials (SCMs) are materials used to substitute clinker in cement production. SCMs do not usually have an elastic demand as they are mostly produced as by-products. Examples of SCMs are: fly ash, by-product of coal-dired electricity, and blast furnace slag, by-product of iron and steel production. According to Scrivener et al. (2018) clay is the material that could supply an increased demand for SCMs.

Scrivener et al. 2018. Calcined clay limestone cements (LC3). Cement and Concrete Research, 114, 49-56. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cemconres.2017.08.017

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, cold, 5 strokes
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, cold, 5 strokes
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, cold, 5 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes it is possible to produce complex shapes. Cold impact extrusion is performed in room temperature. This dataset represents cold impact extrusion of 1 kg of steel with 5 stroke. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but it excludes the steel input and the process of degreasing.

The process is conventional, meaning that it is controlled manually and needs constant supervision. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of brass. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

The product "limestone, milled, loose" represents 1 kg of loose milled limestone. This product consists of limestone that has been crushed and ground into a fine powder, typically transported and stored without being packed into individual containers. Limestone is a naturally occurring mineral that consists principally of calcium carbonate. It is found in many forms and is classified in terms of its origin, chemical composition, structure, and geological formation. This product is modelled as 97% calcite (CaCO3), 2% silica (SiO2) and 1% magnesite (MgCO3). Limestone occurs widely throughout the world and is an essential raw material for many industries. Limestone is generally used for construction, cement, quicklime, agriculture, metal refining and flue gas desulphurisation: Construction: Limestone is the most widely used crushed rock, although it is generally sold in form of sand and gravel. Cement: The production of cement is the main use for chalk, and a major use for dense, high-calcium limestone. Quicklime: Approximately 1.8 t of limestone is required per 1.0 t of quicklime. Agriculture: Many crops grow best under neutral to slightly acid conditions (i.e., pH between 6 and 7). Thus soils which are more acidic than pH 6 generally benefit from the application of limestone. Metal refining: When limestone is used in metal refining it is initially converted to quicklime, which reacts with acidic oxides (e.g., SiO2, Al2O, and Fe2O3) to produce molten slag. Flue gas desulphurisation: A growing use of limestone is in the treatment of flue gas from combustion processes to remove sulphur dioxide.

This is delivering the service of 'injection moulding'. Injection moulding is the most important process for producing moldings from thermoplastics, elastomers, and thermosets. Injection molding is a discontinuous process in which plastic pellets, granules or powder is melted and injected under pressure into the cavity of a mold, where it is solidified by cooling or thermally cross-linked.  In the service of injection moulding, 1 kg of this process corresponds to 0.994 kg of injection moulded plastics. The converted amount of plastics is not included into the dataset. Thus, this service should be used along with 1 kg of thermoplastics, elastomers, and thermosets material.

activity_name: market for quicklime, in pieces, loose
product_information: The product "quicklime, in pieces, loose" represents the production of 1 kg of quicklime in pieces, obtained by calcination of crushed limestone. Quicklime is produced by the thermal decomposition of limestone. It consists mainly of calcium oxide (CaO), but has other components such as silicon dioxide (SiO2) and magnesium oxide (MgO). Its quality depends on many factors, including physical properties, degree of sintering and chemical composition. As the most readily available and cost effective alkali, quicklime plays an essential part in a wide variety of industrial processes. The difference between clinker and quicklime used for products in the binding agent industry is mainly the calcination temperature which is a maximum of 1450ºC for clinker and a maximum of 1200ºC for quicklime. This form of quicklime can then be sent for grinding to obtain milled quicklime. Quicklime is used for soil stabilisation in the road construction industry, as a building material, as a process material in the chemical and sugar industries, in the iron and steel industry where it is used as an additive for the formation of slag in electric furnaces, and for the treatment of wastewater.

activity_name: market for melamine urea formaldehyde adhesive
product_information:
Melamine Urea Formaldehyde adhesive (also MUF adhesive, MUF glue), is a type of adhesive which is applied in the production of engineered wood products, for example in the production of glued laminated timber. 

MUF adhesive is transparent and light resistant. It can be hardened either hot or cold. Compared to UF glue, MUF glue is more elastic. It therefore provides improved resistance to humidity. 
For the production of MUF adhesive, UF adhesive is mixed with melamine.
Urea is produced from the reforming of natural gas into ammonia and the subsequent reaction with CO2. Formaldehyde is produced from the oxidation of methanol, which in turn results from the reforming of natural gas under the use of a catalyst. Melamine is produced from urea under high pressure and temperature and use of a catalyst. CO2 and ammonia are by-products to the melamine production and can be recycled. (Messmer & Chaudhardy, 2015).

Messmer, Annika & Chaudhary, Abhishek. (2015). Life cycle assessment of adhesives used in wood constructions. Masterthesis, ETH Zürich.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, hot, 2 strokes
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, hot, 2 strokes
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, hot, 2 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes it is possible to produce complex shapes. Hot impact extrusion is performed at temperature above the crystallisation point of the processed metal. This dataset represents hot impact extrusion of 1 kg of steel with 2 stroke. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but it excludes the steel input and the process of degreasing.

The product 'thermo-mechanical pulp' (TMP) is a type of unbleached, mechanical, wood-containing pulp. Mechanical pulp is weaker than chemical pulp (sulfate and sulfite pulp) but has a higher light-scattering coefficients and opacity. In the TMP production process, wood chips are thermally pre-treated with steam and defibrated in the refiner. Defibration can happen in a single stage under pressure and increased temperature or in two stages under high pressure (~about 3 bar) or at atmospheric pressure. During mechanical defibration, lignin is plasticised and remains in the pulp. TMP cannot be brightened effectively by oxidative and reductive bleaching. The majority of mechanical pulp is produced at integrated pulp mills which directly process it further into (paper-) products and only a minor part of pulp production is sold on the market. TMP pulp is mainly used for Printing & writing papers and newsprint.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of aluminium, 2 strokes
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of aluminium, 2 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes it is possible to produce complex shapes. This dataset represents the impact extrusion of 1 kg of aluminium with 2 strokes. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but is excludes the input of alumimium and the process of degreasing is not included.
The product 'radish' is a vegetable. It is an annual crop.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, warm, 5 strokes
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, warm, 5 strokes
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, warm, 5 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes is possible, to produce complex shapes. Warm impact extrusion is performed at temperature between hot and cold impact extrusion, since it is a compromise between the two. This dataset represents warm impact extrusion of 1 kg of steel with 5 stroke. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but it excludes the steel input and the process of degreasing.

activity_name: market for chlorine, for reuse in zirconium and hafnium tetrachloride production
reference_product_name: chlorine, for reuse in zirconium and hafnium tetrachloride production
product_information: This product represents chlorine produced by the electrolysis of magnesium chloride according to the following reaction: MgCl2 -> Mg + Cl2. The magnesium chloride is obtained during the production of zirconium sponge using the Kroll process, in the reduction and distillation step, described by the following reaction: ZrCl4 + 2Mg -> Zr + 2MgCl2. Through the electrolysis of magnesium chloride, part of the Mg and Cl is recovered and then reused in the Kroll process. The chlorine is used as input to the carbochlorination and extractive distillation process, which produces zirconium and hafnium tetrachloride according to the following reaction: Zr(Hf)SiO4 + 4C + 4Cl2 = Zr(Hf)Cl4 + SiCl4 + 4CO. This product should not be used in any other context.

Europium is a lanthanide, a rare earth element, that is most commonly found in the oxide minerals monzanite and bastnaesite. The Bayan Obo iron ore deposit is the most largest known deposit of europium and China is the largest supplier of europium oxide (Eu2O3) globally, representing approximately 93% of world production. 'diazine-compound' represents an organic substance. The compounds fragment is a diazine ring (benzene ring where two carbons are replaced with nitrogen atoms) and the molecular formula of the fragment is: e.g. -(CHCHCHCHNN)-. There are three diazine isomers, pyridazine (1,2 diazine, see example before), pyrimidine (1,3 diazine) and pyrazine (1,4 diazine), all with the formula C4H4N2. It is mostly solid (else liquid) under normal conditions of temperature and pressure. The dataset was modelled from the arithmetic mean of all inputs and outputs of the production of several representing substances. The reference product is modelled to represent a pure substance. activity_name: market for solid bleached and unbleached board carton
reference_product_name: solid bleached and unbleached board carton
product_information: The product 'solid bleached and unbleached board carton', also called SBB and SUB, are packaging grades of board used to make cartons. The are made using 100% virgin fibres. SBB is typically made from pure bleached chemical pulp with two or three layers of coating on the top surface and one or more layers on the reverse. There are also uncoated grades. Typical markets include cosmetics, pharmaceuticals, graphics, tobacco, and luxury packaging. SUB is typically made from pure unbleached chemical pulp with two or three layers of coating on the top surface. In some cases, a white reverse surface is applied. It is primarily used as beverage carriers for bottles and cans, as it is very strong and can be made water resistant. It is used where strength of packaging is important. SUB may also be used in the production of liquid beverage cartons and liquid food cartons.

The product " paper, woodcontaining, supercalendered", also called SC paper, is a type of graphic paper. More specific, it is an uncoated, mechanical paper which means that less than 90% of the fibres are in form of chemical pulp. Usually, this kind of paper mill contains an integrated mechanical pulp production and sometimes also an integrated deinking equipment for recovered paper. Used sulfate pulp is usually produced outside of the paper mill. Like lightweight coated (LWC) paper, it is mainly used for print materials such as magazines and journals that require a higher paper quality than newsprint paper.

The seed produced at the farm is transported to the processing centre, treated (pre-cleaning, cleaning, eventually drying, chemical dressing (for integrated production) and bag filling), stored and afterwards transported to the regional storage centre. Swiss integrated production refers to agriculture meeting the ecological requirements (ökologischer Leistungsnachweis, ÖLN) defined by the Swiss regulation on direct payments to agriculture (Direktzahlungsverordnung).

This is delivering the service of 'hot rolling, steel'. Hot-rolling refers to a mill process in which the steel is rolled at a temperature above its recrystallization temperature with a heat that typically exceeds 1000° F. The hot metal is passed between two rolls to flatten it, lengthen it, reduce the cross-sectional area and obtain a uniform thickness. Then the steel is cooled at room temperature.The service includes input materials, energy and water consumption, production infrastructure, emissions and waste treatment, but it excludes the input of steel to be rolled. Hot-rolled steel typically has a scaly surface finish and it suitable for structural components and other applications where incredibly precise shapes and tolerances are of less importance like railroad tracks, I-beams, agricultural equipment, sheet metal and automotive frames.

activity_name: market for steel removed by milling, dressing
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'steel removed by milling, dressing'. Milling is a chipping process where the piece of the material is fixed to a movable table while the cutting energy is provided by the rotating cutting tool. It is used for complex shaping and it can be applied to metals, wood and plastic materials. When the process is performed with dressing operation, then only minor amounts of materials are removed to produce the exact dimensions and smooth surface. This dataset represents the milling of 1kg of steel. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, energy consumption, water consumption and materials end-of-life. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

activity_name: market for steel removed by turning, primarily dressing, computer numerical controlled
reference_product_name: steel removed by turning, primarily dressing, computer numerical controlled
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'steel removed by turning, primarily dressing, computer numerical controlled'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. When the process is performed with dressing operation, then only minor amounts of materials are removed to produce the exact dimensions and smooth surface. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of steel. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

It is waxy solid under normal conditions of temperature and pressure with a molecular weight over 1000 g/mol There are no publicly available information about the consumption of this substance on a consumer level. product_information: The product 'wood chips, wet, measured as dry mass' represent wood chips produced from residual wood (slab and sidings) and logs at the sawmill site as well as wood chips produced in forestry activities. The weight is expressed as dry mass. Wood chips are produced by chopping larger pieces of softwood or hardwood into chips. 'Wood chips, wet, measured as dry mass' are produced with a stationary electric chipper (at the sawmill), with a mobile chipper (at forest road), or with a diesel terrain chipper (in the stand). 'pig iron' is a metal. Pig iron contains about 90-95% of elemental iron together with several other elements mainly carbon, silicon, phosphorus, manganese, and sulfur – in varying proportions depending on the compositions of the raw materials and the mode of an operating blast furnace. It is a transition metal. It is an intermediate product, generated by smelting iron ore in a blast furnace. It can be used in the following applications and/or processes: in an electric arc furnace for steelmaking, in foundries for producing ductile iron castings, in foundry pig iron in grey iron castings.

activity_name: market for aluminium removed by turning, primarily dressing, computer numerical controlled
reference_product_name: aluminium removed by turning, primarily dressing, computer numerical controlled
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'aluminium removed by turning, primarily dressing, computer numerical controlled'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. When the process is performed with dressing operation, then only minor amounts of materials are removed to produce the exact dimensions and smooth surface. The process is computer numerical controlled, meaning that it operates autonomously once programmed. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of aluminium. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

This is delivering the service of 'thermoforming, with calendering'. Thermoforming is the a purely stretching technique of manufacture of three-dimensional mouldings from flat plastic preforms such as films or sheets, under the influence of heat and pressure or vacuum. The range of products that can be manufactured by this process includes packaging containers, bulky moldings such as swimming pools,  automobile components such as dashboards and passenger compartment panels. The preferred materials for processing include amorphous thermoplastics [PVC, polystyrene (PS), acrylonitrile - butadiene - styrene (ABS), styrene - acrylonitrile (SAN), cellulose acetate butyrate (CAB), PMMA, and PC], as well as semicrystalline materials (PP, PE,PETP) and special laminates.  Calendering is a speciality process for high-volume, high quality plastic film and sheet, mainly used for PVC as well as for certain other modified thermoplastics. The melted polymer is subject to heat and pressure in an extruder and formed into sheet or film by calendering rolls.  In the thermoforming with calendering service, 1 kg of this process equal corresponds to kg of thermoformed, calendered plastic sheets. The converted amount of plastics is not included into the dataset. This, this service should be used along with 1 kg of thermoplastic material

The product "clay brick" represents bricks with clay as the main primary material. Ordinary bricks constitute the largest fraction of total brick production. They have a porous body. If there are no special requirements with regard to appearance or resistance to weathering, they are called inner-wall bricks, or backing bricks, and can be solid or hollow. For hollow bricks there are requirements for compression strength (4 - 28 MPa) and brick bulk density of 1.0 -2.2 kg/dm3. (The brick bulk density is calculated by dividing the mass of the dried brick by its volume including all channels, finger holes, and mortar pockets, or frogs.) One special variety of brick is the insulating brick used to reduce heat flow. For these the strength specification is between 2 and 28 MPa. The brick density must be only between 0.6 and 1.0 kg/dm3. Adding materials that burn during firing increases the porosity. Insulating brick can be as large as 49 × 30 × 23.8 cm. Progress is being made in producing room high building components. These are known as brick planks and are produced as monolithic pieces with an extrusion press. If the brick has surface and colour suitable for facing and it is able to withstand frost, the brick is classified as face brick, or facing brick. If the average compression strength is 3 45 MPa, the brick is ranked as extra strong. The main product of the Swiss industry is brick. It is usually plastered. If it is used for a visible brick wall aesthetic criteria as colour and structure of the surface have to be fulfilled and a good weathering resistance is necessary.

activity_name: market for spray-up for composite production, spray-up of resin and fibres
reference_product_name: spray-up for composite production, spray-up of resin and fibres
product_information:
This is delivering the service of "spray-up for composite production, spray-up of resin and fibres". Spray-up is a process for composite transformation product in which resin (polyester) and fibres (glass) will be projected on a mould. Then, it is solidified by polymerisation and evaporation of solvent included into the resin.
In the service of simultaneous projection, 1 kg of this process corresponds to 0.75 kg of the final product at the end of the process. The converted amount of material (fibres and resin) is not included into the dataset. The user should list 1 kg of the service and 1 kg of the resin and fibre INPUT for an OUTPUT of 0.75 kg of material processed. If an activity requires 1 kg of sprayed product, the user should list (i) 1/0.75 = 1.33 kg of the service and 1.33 kg of the resin/fibres type processed.

The product "expanded clay" represents clay expanded by heat. Its production includes mined clay being mixed with water, ground, sometimes granulated and burned at reverse flow in a rotary furnace at 1200 °C. The included organic materials burn and the clay pellets expand with the help of heavy fuel oil. The surface melts and produces a sintered shell. Expanded at 1200 °C in a rotary furnace. Heavy fuel oil is used for heating. Bulk density of expanded clay: 260 - 500kg/m3. Expanded clay is mainly used as a lightweight aggregate for lightweight concrete blocks and thermal plaster. It is also used as sound and thermal bulk insulation.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, cold, tempering
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, cold, tempering
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, cold, tempering'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. To further strengthen and harden the extruded metal, tempering process is used via quenching (cooling), heat treatment, and/or cold working techniques. This process represents the tempering of 1 kg steel and it is considered as an integral part of the cold impact extrusion dataset. It only includes the energy consumption for the heat treatment of the steel after it has been deformed by impact extrusion.

It dissolves in liquid ammonia to give a blue solution. It is modelled as a pure substance. product_information: recycled polyethylene terephthalate (PET) granulates of amorphous

Low-grade (4N) primary gallium is an intermediate product of the gallium chain. It can be produced via a number of different process (e.g. fractional precipitation, mercury cathode electrolysis, solvent extraction etc.) and serves as the primary input for the production of high-purity (6N-7N) gallium. The semi-conductor industry is the primary consumer of refined (6N-7N) gallium, where it is mostly used for the production of advanced semiconductors and LED chips. It can also be used for wireless applications such as radiofrequency devices (in the form of gallium arsenide).

activity_name: market for steel removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled
reference_product_name: steel removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'steel removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. When the process is performed with roughing operation, then large amounts of materials are removed at once. The process is computer numerical controlled, meaning that it operates autonomously once programmed. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of steel. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

activity_name: market for single use paper cup, lid or container, for food packaging, from virgin fibre
reference_product_name: single use paper cup, lid or container, for food packaging, from virgin fibre
product_information: The product 'single use paper cup, lid or container, for food packaging, from virgin fibre' is a type of paper-based tableware for fast food applications. It represents cups for cold and hot beverages, lids for both type of beverage cups, cups for ice creams, and boxes for salads. It is made of virgin fibres, bleached, and coated with plastics. The production process involves multiple stages such as preparing virgin fibres, pulping, bleaching, papermaking, PE coating, followed by a converting process that includes printing, cutting and folding of the final tableware items. The tableware is intended for single-use applications in fast-food establishments, commonly known as Quick Service Restaurants (QSRs).

Melamine is an organic substance with a CAS no. : 108-78-1. It is called '1,3,5-triazine-2,4,6-triamine' under IUPAC naming and its molecular formula is: C3H6N6. Other names are Cyanurotriamide, Cyanuramide, Isomelamine, and Cyanurotriamine. Melamine is at room temperature a fine white powered crystal. Its solubility in water is depending on temperature. Almost 100% of melamine is produced out of urea. Different production techniques can be distinguished. All of them are either high-pressure reactions without any catalysts or catalytic reactions at low pressure. This product represents a 50:50 mix of melamine produced by the BASF process (as representative for the low-pressure reactions) and the Montedison process (example of high pressure process). Most melamine is used for the reaction with formaldehyde for the production of resins used in laminating and adhesive applications. Among these applications, one of the most important ones is the use of melamine for the upper sheet e. g. of tabletops. A second important use of melamine is its function as amino cross-linker in heat-cured systems - e. g. in the automotive industry. Furthermore, this substance is also applied in other fields like wet-strength resins for paper industry, ion-exchange resins, plastic moulding compounds or fire retardants in PUR foam.

activity_name: market for cast iron removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled
reference_product_name: cast iron removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'cast iron removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. When the process is performed with roughing operation, then large amounts of materials are removed at once. The process is computer numerical controlled, meaning that it operates autonomously once programmed. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of cast iron. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

The inventory for the glass fibre is based on a literature study for the technologies used in the European glass industry. The energy use is the main environmental aspect. Glass fibres can be used in manifold ways. Therefore resins and different chemicals are necessary. In ecoinvent, there are two types of processing, hand lay-up moulding with polyester resin and injection moulding with polyamide. The unit process raw data are meant to be used as background information. For other types of moulded products the background data for glass fibre can be used to assess the environmental impacts if the amount of other chemicals and the process requirements are known.

reference_product_name: electric arc furnace secondary metallurgy slag, for recovery
product_information: Slag is a by-product of the steelmaking process that is produced during the smelting process. Slags are principally composed of impurities in the charge and inert materials that are added in the beginning of the steelmaking process. Ladle furnace (LF) slags are typically recovered for use internally or externally (e.g. as an aggregate), although a portion may be landfilled or stockpilled. This represents high grade material that is sent for recovery.

activity_name: market for brass removed by drilling, conventional
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'brass removed by drilling, conventional'. Drilling uses a rotating cutting tool (the drill bit) to remove material from the work piece and it predominately uses the front end for cutting. It is applied for shapes like circular cavity or holes. The process is conventional, meaning that it is controlled manually and needs constant supervision. This dataset represents the drilling of 1 kg of brass. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Maintenance and tool (bit) replacements are not included. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

![image](https://db3.ecoinvent.org/images/4a10e256-b928-4cc4-a8e4-16a78446a17f)

The product "gypsum fibreboard" represents gypsum fibreboard produced 100% from natural gypsum (no FGD). Gypsum fibre boards are produced in the following way: The gypsum is mixed with water, fibres and additives and evenly spread on the belt of a calender. The board is pressed and when the gypsum is set, the edges are cut and the surplus water is removed by drying for about one hour in a kiln. Finally the boards are cut and packed. Gypsum fibre board consists of gypsum with 15-20% cellulose fibres. This product has a density of 1000 - 1250 kg/m3 and a thermal conductivity of 0.36 W/mK. Gypsum boards have good sound-absorbing properties and because of the constitutional water they show a good fire resistance and consequently often are used where these properties are required.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, warm, 1 strokes
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, warm, 1 strokes
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, warm, 1 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes is possible, to produce complex shapes. Warm impact extrusion is performed at temperature between hot and cold impact extrusion, since it is a compromise between the two. This dataset represents warm impact extrusion of 1 kg of steel with 1 stroke. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but it excludes the steel input and the process of degreasing.

This service allows the users to model the production of different carton board boxes according to their needs. Per kg of used carton board, 0.83 kg of this module are required.

activity_name: market for aluminium removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled
reference_product_name: aluminium removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'aluminium removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. When the process is performed with roughing operation, then large amounts of materials are removed at once. The process is computer numerical controlled, meaning that it operates autonomously once programmed. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of aluminium. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

This process describes the production of 1kg of barium hydroxide. It is primarily used to produce organic barium compounds, which are employed for example as additives for oil and stabilizers for plastics (Kresse et al., 2007). Another area of application comprises dehydration and deacidification, particularly when it comes to removing sulfuric acid from oils, fats, waxes and glycerol (Kresse et al., 2007). In specific cases of applications barium hydroxide can also replace barium carbonate. 

Reference(s): Kresse, R., Baudis, U., Jäger, P., Riechers, H. H., Wagner, H., Winkler, J. and Wolf, H. U. 2007. Barium and Barium Compounds. Ullmann's Encyclopedia of Industrial Chemistry. pp. 15-16. Wiley-VCH, Weinheim.

activity_name: market for magnesium, for reuse in hafnium sponge production
reference_product_name: magnesium, for reuse in hafnium sponge production
product_information: This product represents magnesium produced by the electrolysis of magnesium chloride according to the following reaction: MgCl2 -> Mg + Cl2. The magnesium chloride is obtained during the production of hafnium sponge using the Kroll process, in the reduction and distillation step, described by the following reaction: HfCl4 + 2Mg -> Hf + 2MgCl2. Through the electrolysis of magnesium chloride, part of the Mg and Cl is recovered and then reused in the Kroll process. This product should not be used in any other context.

This is delivering the service of 'stretch blow moulding'. Stretch blow molding is a special form of blow molding. The process exploits the fact that high degrees of orientation can be introduced in plastics by drawing at temperatures near the glass transition temperature or crystalline melting point. Mechanical properties are considerably improved in this way. In this process, the preform is drawn not only in the circumferential direction (as with extrusion blow molding) but also in the machine direction. The longitudinal drawing is brought about mechanically by using a ram. Because of the relatively low temperature at which this process is carried out, high deformation forces are required (air pressure up to 2 MPa).  In the service of strech blow moulding, 1 kg of this process corresponds to 0.978 kg of strech blow moulded plastic.The converted amount of plastics is not included into the dataset. Thus, this service should be used along with 1 kg of thermoplastic materials, like polyvinyl chloride (PVC), polypropylene (PP), and polyethylene terephthalate (PETP).

activity_name: market for chromium steel removed by turning, primarily roughing, conventional
reference_product_name: chromium steel removed by turning, primarily roughing, conventional
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'chromium steel removed by turning, primarily roughing, conventional'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. When the process is performed with roughing operation, then large amounts of materials are removed at once. The process is conventional, meaning that it is controlled manually and needs constant supervision. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of chromium steel. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

The product "synthesis gas, 2 to 1"  is a gaseous mixture of H2 and CO, which may include other compounds as impurities (e.g., H2O, CH4, and CO2). The product, synthesis gas, 2 to 1, represents gaseous mixtures with a hydrogen to carbon monoxide ratio of 2. Synthesis gas is a key chemical intermediate in many relevant processes including, but not limited to, the production of (i) methanol (ii) synthetic fuels via  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis, and (iii)  hydrogen. This product is a green glass that is used as a packaging material. Its production can be of virgin input materials or a mix of primary and secondary raw materials.

The product 'containerboard, linerboard' is a specific type of board. Containerboard is the umbrella term for linerboard and fluting medium, which are both required to produce corrugated board. Linerboard is a flat layer of board, which can be further distinguished in kraftliner and testliner, depending on whether it is of virgin or recycled origin, respectively. Fluting medium is the corrugated middle layer between a top and bottom layer of linerboard. Together all three layers constitute corrugated board. This product is a mix of virgin and recycled liner board.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, cold, initial surface treatment
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, cold, initial surface treatment
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, cold, initial surface treatment'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. Surface treatment is a part of these process, including bonderizing and degreasing of the metal in room temperature. This dataset represents the surface treatment of 1 kg of steel and it is considered as an integral part of the cold impact extrusion dataset. It includes only the energy consumption and it can be applied no matter how may strokes the process includes.

activity_name: market for wood preservation, dipping/immersion method, water-based, indoor use, dry
reference_product_name: wood preservation, dipping/immersion method, water-based, indoor use, dry
product_information: This is delivering the service of wood preservation with the water-based dipping/immersion method. The service represents the surface treatment of dried wood with 1 kg of diluted water-based wood preservative for use class 1 (for indoor, dry use). The treatment takes place in open tank impregnation with the wood preservatives to penetrate the wood no more than three millimetres deep. The process includes inputs of the wood preservative, electricity consumption, infrastructure, emissions and end-of-life. The input of wood is excluded and should be added manually. A typical application amount is 200-250 g/m2 for preventive and 300-350 g/m2 for curative use case. This means that for treating 1 m2 wood preventively, 0.20-0.25 kg of this dataset is required.

activity_name: market for sputtering target, sintered, indium tin oxide
reference_product_name: sputtering target, sintered, indium tin oxide
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'sputtering target, sintered, indium tin oxide'. Sputtering target is a physical vapor deposition mechanism that is used to deposit a thin film of a substance in a given surface of an object. This dataset represents the sputtering target of indium tin oxide (ITO) bound to a copper backplate with dimensions of 610mm x 350mm. activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, cold, 4 strokes
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, cold, 4 strokes
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, cold, 4 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes it is possible to produce complex shapes. Cold impact extrusion is performed in room temperature. This dataset represents cold impact extrusion of 1 kg of steel with 4 stroke. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but it excludes the steel input and the process of degreasing.

There are a very wide range of cheese varieties and many subtle differences in processing methods. In general, however, the following process steps apply: production of coagulum through the action of rennet and/or lactic acid, separation of the resulting curds from the whey, and manipulation of the curds to produce the desired characteristics of the cheese. Cheese is also a by-product of yogurt production.

This is in addition to the materials used for maintenance.  A user wishing to model this infrastructure in their studies, is advised to examine the relevant ecoinvent report (ecoinvent report No. 15.)

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, cold, 3 strokes
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, cold, 3 strokes
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, cold, 3 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes it is possible to produce complex shapes. Cold impact extrusion is performed in room temperature. This dataset represents cold impact extrusion of 1 kg of steel with 3 stroke. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but it excludes the steel input and the process of degreasing.

activity_name: market for wood preservation, dipping/immersion method, organic solvent-based, indoor use, occasionally wet
reference_product_name: wood preservation, dipping/immersion method, organic solvent-based, indoor use, occasionally wet
product_information: This is delivering the service of wood preservation with the organic solvent-based dipping/immersion method. The service represents the surface treatment of dried wood with 1 kg of wood preservative for use class 2 (for indoor, occasionally wet use). The treatment takes place in open tank impregnation with the wood preservatives to penetrate the wood no more than three millimetres deep. The process includes inputs of the wood preservative, electricity consumption, infrastructure, emissions and end-of-life. The input of wood is excluded and should be added manually. A typical application amount is 200-250 g/m2. This means that for treating 1 m2 wood, 0.20-0.25 kg of this dataset is required.

activity_name: market for thermoforming of plastic sheets
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'thermoforming of plastic sheets'. Thermoforming is the a purely stretching technique of manufacture of three-dimensional mouldings from flat plastic preforms such as films or sheets, under the influence of heat and pressure or vacuum. The range of products that can be manufactured by this process includes packaging containers, bulky moldings such as swimming pools,  automobile components such as dashboards and passenger compartment panels. The preferred materials for processing include amorphous thermoplastics [PVC, polystyrene (PS), acrylonitrile - butadiene - styrene (ABS), styrene - acrylonitrile (SAN), cellulose acetate butyrate (CAB), PMMA, and PC], as well as semicrystalline materials (PP, PE,PETP) and special laminates. This service activity represents thermoforming of plastic sheets of 200 microns to 2 mm thickness in plastic packaging for the food industry. The activity refers to an input of 1 kg of plastic sheets but the amount of plastic processed is not included. Thus, this service should be used along with 1 kg of suitable material. The preferred materials for processing include amorphous thermoplastics [PVC, polystyrene (PS), acrylonitrile - butadiene - styrene (ABS), styrene - acrylonitrile (SAN), cellulose acetate butyrate (CAB), PMMA, and PC], as well as semicrystalline materials (PP, PE,PETP) and special laminates that economically combine the favorable properties of several polymers in one film.

This is delivering the service of 'wire drawing, steel'. Wire drawing is a process in which wire rods/wires are reduced in diameter by drawing them through cone-shaped openings of a smaller cross section, so called dies. The input usually is wire rod of diameters raging from 5.5 to 16 mm obtained from hot rolling mills in form of coils. The service includes materials inputs, energy and water consumption, infrastructure, emissions and waste but it excludes the steel input to be rolled and coating. Wire drawing`s main applications include electrical wiring, cables, tension-loaded structural components, springs, paper clips, spokes for wheels, and stringed musical instruments, etc.

reference_product_name: shavings, softwood, measured as dry mass
product_information: Shavings are a by-product of the planing process where they are generated as loose shavings. 'Shavings, softwood, measured as dry mass' represent shavings which have been transported via aspiration (suction) and collected at a silo at the sawmill site. The amount is expressed as dry mass. The collection of shavings by suction (aspiration) takes place at the sawmill site. In the ecoinvent database, 'shavings, softwood, measured as dry mass' are used for the production of wood pellets.

To produce woven bamboo mats, a bamboo pole is first flattened and then divided into strips with widths between 2 and 4 cm. These strips are then peeled into 1- to 2-mm-thick veneers, which are then woven to form a mat. The entire process is manual and is typically performed in small, rural communities. The woven bamboo mats are usually used as lightweight walls but have also recently been used for industrially produced panels (Xiao, 2009).
 

Reference:
Xiao, Y., 2009. Development of Prefabricated Bamboo Earthquake Relief Shelter. In:
International Conference of Modern Bamboo Structures. Bogota, Colombia.

activity_name: market for extrusion of plastic sheets and thermoforming, inline
reference_product_name: extrusion of plastic sheets and thermoforming, inline
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'extrusion of plastic sheets and thermoforming, inline'. Extrusion describes the continuous manufacture of a semifinished plastic product (film, sheet, pipe, profile). An extrusion line consists of the following: 1. Extruder 2. Mold or die 3. Calibration and cooling 4. Takeoff 5. Finishing. Extrusion is combined with thermoforming. Thermoforming is the a purely stretching technique of manufacture of three-dimensional mouldings from flat plastic preforms such as films or sheets, under the influence of heat and pressure or vacuum. The range of products that can be manufactured by this process includes packaging containers, bulky moldings such as swimming pools,  automobile components such as dashboards and passenger compartment panels. The preferred materials for processing include amorphous thermoplastics [PVC, polystyrene (PS), acrylonitrile - butadiene - styrene (ABS), styrene - acrylonitrile (SAN), cellulose acetate butyrate (CAB), PMMA, and PC], as well as semicrystalline materials (PP, PE,PETP) and special laminates.  The dataset combines the services of extrusion and thermoforming refers of plastic sheets, inline. The inventory refers to an input of 1 kg of plastic pellets but the amount of plastic pellets processed is not included.

It is the direct product from mining and beneficiation and a by-product from gold mine operation and mining. It is in a bullion form. It can be used in the following applications and/or processes: batteries, pigments manufacturing, glass and ceramics production, as a additive in paints, as plastic stabilizer, electrical and telecommunication cables and wires, metal alloys, gasoline additive, shot and ammunition substance, etc.

The product "anhydrite rock" represents the natural anhydrite rock, milled (comprising mostly of calcium sulphate, CaSO4). It is mined together with gypsum in open pit mining. The intermediate products are composed of about 65% natural gypsum (CaSO4 * 2 H2O), 34% natural anhydrite rock (CaSO4) and 1% soil and gravel that are deposited back in the mine.  The material is gained by blasting and digging. The output of the mine is crushed and sorted. Anhydrite rock is used by the cement industry; it has to be finely ground for use as cast plaster floor.

activity_name: market for magnesium chloride, from hafnium sponge production
reference_product_name: magnesium chloride, from hafnium sponge production
product_information:
"This product represents magnesium chloride that is produced during the production of hafnium sponge using the Kroll process. The reduction and distillation step consists of the following reaction: HfCl4 + 2Mg -> Hf + 2MgCl2. 
 The magnesium chloride produced from this reaction is sent to an electrolysis process, which recovers part of the magnesium and chlorine. This product should not be used in any other context."

activity_name: market for white lined chipboard carton
product_information: White Lined Chipboard WLC (also known as GT /GD /UD) is a packaging grade of board used to manufacture cartons. It is typically made using predominantly recovered paper or recovered fibres, but may incoroprate some virgin fibres. It is manufactured in a number of layers, each of which use selected grades of raw materials. It may have up to three layers of coating on the top or printing surface and one layer on the reverse.  It is used in a range of applications such as frozen and chilled foods, cereals, shoes, tissues, toys. The board is converted into a carton at a converting site which typically includes a printing process and cutting/creasing of the board into carton blanks, ready for delivery to and erection by the packer/filler.

The product "tube insulation, elastomere" represents rubber foam pipes. Tube insulation materials can be used for example as insulation material for warm water pipes of solar collectors. The production process can be divided into pre-production involving a synthetic rubber mass being kneaded and rolled out to rubber basic furs, and the finishing process including vulcanisation of the tubes. The product is cut to size, labelled and packed in cardboard. Most (82%) of the production wastes are recycled within the same production process. The specific weight is estimated with 75kg/m3. This product does not include the end-of-life of materials used to construct it.

product_information: Slag is a by-product of the steelmaking process that is produced during the smelting process. This represents high grade material that is sent for recovery.

activity_name: market for aluminium removed by milling, large parts
reference_product_name: aluminium removed by milling, large parts
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'aluminium removed by milling, large parts'. Milling is a chipping process where the piece of the material is fixed to a movable table while the cutting energy is provided by the rotating cutting tool. It is used for complex shaping and it can be applied to metals, wood and plastic materials. This dataset represents the milling of 1kg of aluminium in large parts. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, energy consumption, water consumption and materials end-of-life. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

The product "bitumen seal, Alu80" represents a bitumen sealing consisting of bitumen (major input in terms of mass), a fleece layer (glass fibre, polyester, glass etc.), limestone and schist meal, as well as sand and talcum. The product is applicable in the construction building sector. Bitumen sealing is used to protect the construction against water intrusion, mainly in flat roofed houses and in road construction work. The bitumen sealing types described in ecoinvent are V60, Alu80, VA4 and EP4 flam.

The product 'cement, CEM IV/A' represents cement (CEM IV/A) according to EN 197-1, also known as pozzolanic cement. CEM II/A is a blended cement that is made by partially replacing 11-35% of Portland cement with fly ash, which is a waste from power stations, and pozzolana (weight-%). Natural pozzolanas include volcanic ashes, opaline shales, and cherts that contain reactive compounds of silicon and/or aluminium. Fly ash is a synthetic pozzolana.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, cold, deformation stroke
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, cold, deformation stroke
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, cold, deformation stroke'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. The use of multiple strokes is possible, to produce complex shapes. Strokes deformation is of minor importance when only a single stroke is needed. However, a piece with multiple strokes can significally increase the energy intensity of the process. This dataset represents the stroke deformation of 1 kg steel and it only includes the electricity consumption of the machine during the deformation .

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, cold, 2 strokes
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, cold, 2 strokes
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, cold, 2 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes it is possible to produce complex shapes. Cold impact extrusion is performed in room temperature. This dataset represents cold impact extrusion of 1 kg of steel with 2 stroke. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but it excludes the steel input and the process of degreasing.

reference_product_name: steel removed by turning, average, conventional
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'steel removed by turning, average, conventional'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. The process is conventional, meaning that it is controlled manually and needs constant supervision. The process is conventional, meaning that it is controlled manually and needs constant supervision. product_information: This is delivering the service of 'brass removed by drilling, computer numerical controlled'. Drilling uses a rotating cutting tool (the drill bit) to remove material from the work piece and it predominately uses the front end for cutting. The process is computer numerical controlled, meaning that it operates autonomously once programmed. This dataset represents the drilling of 1 kg of brass. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

activity_name: market for aluminium removed by turning, primarily roughing, conventional
reference_product_name: aluminium removed by turning, primarily roughing, conventional
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'aluminium removed by turning, primarily roughing, conventional'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. When the process is performed with roughing operation, then large amounts of materials are removed at once. The process is conventional, meaning that it is controlled manually and needs constant supervision. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of aluminium. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

The product 'cork slab' represents cork agglomerates glued together with melamine, urea-formaldehyde, and phenolic resin. The cork for the agglomerated slab is triturated and sieved. The cork granules are pressed to a slab together with the agglomeration resin. This can be done in a continuous or in a batch process. Finally, the slab is sanded and stored for stabilisation. Cork slab is used e. g. as underlay for floating floorings or as insulation material. This product represents an average of different cork floorings and cork underlays.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of aluminium, cold, tempering
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of aluminium, cold, tempering
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of aluminium, cold, tempering'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure.  To further strengthen and harden the extruded metal, tempering process is used via quenching (cooling), heat treatment, and/or cold working techniques. This process represents the tempering of 1 kg aluminium and it is considered as an integral part of the cold impact extrusion dataset. It only includes the energy consumption for the heat treatment of the aluminium after it has been deformed by impact extrusion.

This is an electric component usually soldered to a printed wiring board (PWB) in electronic circuits. A potentiometer is a variable resistor. Potentiometers are usually used to control the level of analog signals such as for volume control in audio equipment. activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, hot, 3 strokes
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, hot, 3 strokes
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, hot, 3 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes it is possible to produce complex shapes. Hot impact extrusion is performed at temperature above the crystallisation point of the processed metal. This dataset represents hot impact extrusion of 1 kg of steel with 3 stroke. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but it excludes the steel input and the process of degreasing.

activity_name: market for steel, 3.2% silicon alloy, for grain oriented electrical steel
reference_product_name: steel, 3.2% silicon alloy, for grain oriented electrical steel
product_information: steel, 3.2% silicon alloy, for grain oriented electrical steel' is a metal. This is a steel alloy which contains 3.2% of silicon. It is produced combining unalloyed steel with ferrosilicon in order to increase the share of silicon in the molten metal. Once it is casted, it undergoes to a rolling process to have a coil as a finite product. The presence of silicon allows the have high magnetic permeability. The high permeability allows this alloy to be used in cores of electric motors, generators, and more generally electrical machines. The main use is in application where the magnetization plan is not static but it moves. Since the plan is moving, there is not a preferencial direction, so the grain doesn't need to be aligned.

activity_name: market for chromium steel removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled
reference_product_name: chromium steel removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'chromium steel removed by turning, primarily roughing, computer numerical controlled'. Turning is a chipping process where the piece of material is rotating while the cutting tool is not. It can be applied to both inside and outside of the piece to be machined and it is normally used to produce cylindrical shapes. It is performed in metals, wood and plastic materials. When the process is performed with roughing operation, then large amounts of materials are removed at once. The process is computer numerical controlled, meaning that it operates autonomously once programmed. This dataset represents the turning of 1 kg of chromium steel. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, machinery and electricity consumption. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, cold, 1 strokes
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, cold, 1 strokes
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, cold, 1 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes it is possible to produce complex shapes. Cold impact extrusion is performed in room temperature. This dataset represents cold impact extrusion of 1 kg of steel with 1 stroke. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but it excludes the steel input and the process of degreasing.

The product "expanded vermiculite" represents vermiculite expanded by the heat from light fuel oil in 5 - 8 s at 1250 - 1500 °C and then cooled immediately to 400 °C. The volume increase is 15 - 30 fold. Density of the expanded vermiculite is 64 - 160 kg/m3. Vermiculite is used in its expanded state to make use of its low density, good thermal and noise insulating properties, and high absorption capacity for liquids. Application areas include: loose filling for hollow spaces, e.g., cavity walls; production of insulating plaster by bonding with gypsum or cement ; cement-bonded material is also used to prevent condensation on ceilings and walls in rooms with moist atmospheres; waterglass-bonded slabs for covering load-bearing constructional units made of steel or concrete, and for providing a fireproof covering; as an additive to produce lightweight aggregate concrete, in competition with porous and pumice-stone concrete.

activity_name: market for impact extrusion of steel, hot, 1 strokes
reference_product_name: impact extrusion of steel, hot, 1 strokes
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'impact extrusion of steel, hot, 1 strokes'. Impact extrusion is a manufacturing process that the product is made out of metal slug. It is a type of pressure forming that take advantage of the change in hardness of materials under high pressure. A pressure force of typically 3.2 to 10 MN is applied which leads to yielding of the materials and a forming of the slug according to the mould`s shape. The use of multiple strokes it is possible to produce complex shapes. Hot impact extrusion is performed at temperature above the crystallisation point of the processed metal. This dataset represents hot impact extrusion of 1 kg of steel with 1 stroke. The service includes impact extrusion processes, production infrastructure, machinery but it excludes the steel input and the process of degreasing.

This product is an infrastructure, modelling the production, maintenance and end-of-life of an 'tractor, 4-wheel, agricultural'. activity_name: market for steel removed by milling, large parts
reference_product_name: steel removed by milling, large parts
product_information: This is delivering the service of 'steel removed by milling, large parts'. Milling is a chipping process where the piece of the material is fixed to a movable table while the cutting energy is provided by the rotating cutting tool. It is used for complex shaping and it can be applied to metals, wood and plastic materials. This dataset represents the milling of 1kg of steel in large parts. The service includes materials input, production infrastructure, energy consumption, water consumption and materials end-of-life. Degreasing is not included and has to be added if necessary.

Flattened bamboo is produced in a handcrafted process usually done with hatchets. To produce flattened bamboo, a bamboo pole is cracked open and its internodes are removed. The innermost part of the bamboo is then trimmed down. During this process, some fibres are broken, rendering the material flexible but still able to maintain its shape. The main application of flattened bamboo is in load-bearing wall systems, where it is used between bamboo poles to support the soil-cement mortar with which the walls are plastered (Murphy et al., 2004; Zea Escamilla et al., 2013). Flattened bamboo is also used to produce woven bamboo mats. 
References: 
Murphy, R.J., Trujillo, D., Londoño, X., 2004. Life cycle assessment (LCA) of a Guadua house. In: International Symposium of Bamboo e Guadua. Pereira, Colombia.

Zea Escamilla, E. and G. Habert, Environmental impacts from the production of bamboo based construction materials representing the global production diversity. Journal of Cleaner Production, 2013

The product "gypsum plasterboard" represents plasterboard produced 100% from natural gypsum (no FGD). Gypsum plaster board is a sandwich construction with a gypsum core and a cardboard layer on each side. The weight of the cardboard is about 5% of the board. Gypsum plaster boards are produced continuously. The gypsum is mixed with water and additives and evenly spread on the lower cardboard. The upper cardboard is then applied and the edge profile is formed. When the gypsum is set, the surplus water is removed by drying for about one hour in a kiln. Finally the boards are cut and packed. The main application of this product is in lightweight construction.
